1 # Plan Sections 2 3 This reference describes what makes a great implementation plan. It does NOT 4 prescribe how the plan looks on the page — rendering is handled by the 5 format-specific references (`markdown-rendering.md`, `html-rendering.md`). 6 7 ## The outcome 8 9 A great plan enables three audiences to act: 10 11 - **The implementing agent** (`ce-work` or a human) starts from an informed 12 baseline — load-bearing decisions are named, research breadcrumbs orient 13 their own investigation, unit boundaries are clear. The plan gives the 14 implementer a starting point, not a substitute for their own investigation. 15 - **The reviewer** identifies the load-bearing decisions and the boundaries 16 of what's being changed in one pass. 17 - **The future reader** (anyone returning months later) traces why the work 18 was done, what shaped it, and where the artifacts live. 19 20 Sections earn their place by serving one of these audiences. Omit padding. 21 22 ## Decide whether a plan doc is warranted at all 23 24 Not every invocation of `ce-plan` should produce a plan document. For 25 genuinely atomic work, the doc is ceremony — the implementer (whether 26 `ce-work` or a human) can act directly without IDed units, KTDs, or 27 Requirements as a checklist. 28 29 **Bias toward producing a plan.** The risk asymmetry favors writing one: 30 a thin plan doc for small work is mild ceremony, but skipping a plan when 31 one was warranted costs the implementer real time (reinvented decisions, 32 lost unit boundaries, no IDed requirements to verify against). When unsure, 33 write the plan. 34 35 **Skip plan creation only when ALL of these hold:** 36 37 - The work is **atomic** — fits in one commit, no meaningful unit boundaries 38 to break out independently. 39 - There are **no design choices that constrain implementation** — no 40 Key Technical Decisions worth recording. If the work needs the implementer 41 to make a choice between two approaches, those approaches are KTDs and 42 a plan is warranted. 43 - There are **no scope boundaries worth pinning** in writing — the work 44 scope is self-evident from the user's request. 45 - **No upstream artifact** (a brainstorm with R-IDs, an incident report, 46 a deferred-follow-up item from a prior plan) needs traceability through 47 this plan. 48 49 **Stress test the "looks atomic" case.** Many requests look atomic at first 50 glance but hide design decisions: 51 52 - *"Add caching to this endpoint"* — sounds atomic, but TTL, invalidation, 53 cache key shape, and backend selection are all KTDs. Write the plan. 54 - *"Migrate from package A to package B"* — sounds mechanical, but 55 semantic differences between the packages create migration KTDs. Write 56 the plan. 57 - *"Add rate limiting"* — sounds small, but algorithm, scope, and 58 configurability are all KTDs. Write the plan. 59 60 vs. genuine skip cases: 61 62 - *"Fix typo in README line 47"* — atomic, no KTDs, skip the plan. 63 - *"Rename `oldFn` to `newFn` across the repo"* — mechanical, no design 64 choices, skip the plan. 65 - *"Bump dependency X to v2.3.1"* — mechanical, skip the plan (unless the 66 bump introduces breaking changes that warrant unit-by-unit migration). 67 68 When skipping the plan doc, the work proceeds directly to `ce-work` or to 69 implementation, and any decisions made along the way land in the commit 70 message or `docs/solutions/` if they're worth carrying forward. 71 72 ## Hard floor 73 74 When a plan doc is warranted, these sections are present. They carry the 75 contracts downstream consumers depend on. 76 77 - **Summary** — what the plan proposes, in 1-3 lines. Forward-looking; orients 78 the reader before they invest in detail. 79 - **Problem Frame** — why the work is being done. Backward-looking / 80 situational. May merge with Summary for compact plans where the motivation 81 is a single sentence. 82 - **Requirements** (with stable R-IDs) — what must be true after the work 83 ships. Reviewer's checklist; downstream code review verifies against these. 84 - **Key Technical Decisions** (KTDs) — the load-bearing choices that constrain 85 implementation. Each entry is `: `. Without these, the 86 implementer can't tell which design choices are open and which are pinned. 87 - **Implementation Units** (with stable U-IDs) — the discrete units of work, 88 sized so each is independently landable. `ce-work` consumes these to 89 execute. For trivial single-step plans the work may collapse into Summary 90 prose and U-IDs may be omitted; this is rare. 91 92 ## Include when material 93 94 These sections are present when they carry information that isn't covered 95 elsewhere. The test is not "is this a substantial plan?" — it is 96 *"does this specific plan have content this section would surface?"* Filling 97 a section with placeholder prose is worse than omitting it. 98 99 - **High-Level Technical Design** — include when the technical approach has 100 shape that prose alone doesn't carry well: architecture across components, 101 sequencing across processes, state machines, branching gates. 102 Visualizations (component topology, sequence, swim lane, flowchart, 103 data-flow) typically live here. Skip when the approach is a one-paragraph 104 pattern application that the prose itself conveys. 105 106 - **Scope Boundaries** — include when scope is contested, when there are 107 tempting non-goals worth naming explicitly, or when "deferred for later" 108 needs distinguishing from "outside the product's identity." Skip when scope 109 is obvious from Requirements alone. 110 111 - **Open Questions** — include when there are genuinely unresolved items that 112 block planning or implementation. Skip when the plan is complete; an empty 113 "Open Questions: none" section signals false uncertainty. 114 115 - **System-Wide Impact** — include when the change affects cross-cutting 116 concerns (data lifecycles, auth boundaries, performance posture, cardinal 117 rules, shared infrastructure). Skip for changes localized to one component 118 where the impact is self-evident. 119 120 - **Risks & Dependencies** — include when there are real risks worth flagging 121 (external service changes, version pins under churn, behavioral assumptions 122 worth highlighting) or material upstream dependencies. Skip for low-risk 123 localized work. 124 125 - **Acceptance Examples** — include when any requirement has a state-dependent 126 or conditional shape ("When X, Y") where the prose alone leaves ambiguity 127 about edge cases. Skip when all requirements are unconditional and 128 unambiguous. 129 130 - **Documentation / Operational Notes** — include when documentation, 131 monitoring, runbooks, or rollout steps need explicit notes. Skip when the 132 work is purely internal and uses existing operational scaffolding without 133 modification. 134 135 - **Sources / Research** — surface the research that orients the implementer 136 or justifies load-bearing choices. The test: *"if I were the implementer 137 reading this cold, would this breadcrumb help me make better choices?"* 138 Yes → surface (code locations like `services/convex/reports.ts:174-176`, 139 external docs, RFCs, constraints, prior plans — the category is inclusive, 140 not enumerated). Process exhaust (reading the user's prompt, glancing at 141 obvious entry points, restating prose) → omit. Surface inline next to the 142 KTD or unit it justifies, or as a dedicated section — both shapes work. 143 144 ## Agent agency 145 146 The catalog is a floor, not a ceiling. When the plan's content doesn't fit 147 any catalog section, introduce a new one — don't force the content into a 148 section it doesn't belong in. Content drives section choices, not vice 149 versa. 150 151 The agent also picks per artifact: 152 153 - Whether Problem Frame merges into Summary 154 - Sub-groupings (Requirements by capability, KTDs by component, Units phased 155 into milestones) 156 - How much detail each section carries 157 - Whether HTD has one diagram, several, or none — and whether visualizations 158 live in HTD or embedded in other sections 159 160 ## Plan metadata fields 161 162 Every plan carries a small set of stable metadata fields that downstream 163 tooling depends on. The contract is format-independent: in markdown these 164 fields appear as YAML frontmatter at the top of the file; in HTML they 165 appear as visible header text (typically a `
` of `
`/`
` pairs or 166 a stats strip). Field names and semantics are the same across both formats 167 so consumers can locate them without knowing which format produced the 168 plan. 169 170 ### Required 171 172 - **`title`** — verbatim plan title. Matches the H1 (markdown) or document 173 `

` (HTML) so file metadata and visible heading don't drift. 174 - **`type`** — conventional-commit-prefix-aligned classification (`feat`, 175 `fix`, `refactor`, `chore`, `docs`, `perf`, `test`, etc.). Carries the 176 intent the eventual commit message should reflect. 177 - **`status`** — `active` on creation; `ce-work` flips to `completed` on 178 ship. `ce-plan`'s Phase 0.1 resume fast path keys on `active`. In HTML, 179 status MUST render as `{value}` so the flip 180 mechanic can locate and rewrite it by selector (see 181 `references/html-rendering.md`). 182 - **`date`** — creation date in ISO 8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`), ASCII digits only. 183 184 ### Optional but well-known 185 186 These fields are not required, but when set they have fixed names and 187 semantics so downstream tooling can rely on them: 188 189 - **`origin`** — repo-relative path to an upstream brainstorm requirements 190 doc (e.g., `docs/brainstorms/2026-05-12-pagination-requirements.md`). 191 Set when planning from an upstream brainstorm; carried for traceability 192 and re-resolved when `ce-plan` re-deepens. The HITL Proof flow uses 193 `origin` to trace back to the source brainstorm. 194 - **`deepened`** — ISO 8601 date marking the first time the confidence 195 check substantively strengthened the plan. Presence affects Phase 0.1 196 resume fast-path logic (see `references/deepening-workflow.md`). 197 198 Field names are stable across plan revisions — never rename a field or 199 repurpose its semantics. Agents composing new plans MUST use these exact 200 names; adding new fields is fine, but renaming `status` to `state` or 201 `origin` to `source` breaks the downstream consumers above. 202 203 ## ID and content rules 204 205 These apply regardless of rendering format. 206 207 - **Stable IDs.** R-IDs (Requirements), U-IDs (Implementation Units), A-IDs 208 (if Actors fire), F-IDs (if Flows fire), AE-IDs (if Acceptance Examples 209 fire). IDs are stable across plan revisions — never renumber to "clean 210 up gaps." 211 - **Plain prefix.** `R1.`, `U1.` as bullet prefixes. Do not bold; the prefix 212 is visually distinctive on its own. 213 - **Repo-relative paths.** Always. Never absolute paths in plan content; 214 they break portability across machines, worktrees, teammates. 215 - **No process exhaust.** No "captured at Phase X" notes, no `## Next Steps` 216 pointing to the next skill, no italic provenance lines. Engineering process 217 metadata belongs in commit messages and tool output, not the artifact. 218 - **Group Requirements by concern when they span distinct logical areas.** 219 The trigger is distinct concerns, not item count — even four requirements 220 benefit from grouping if they cover three different topics. Skip grouping 221 only when all requirements are genuinely about the same thing; a long flat 222 list is a smell that subgroups were missed. Group by capability (e.g., 223 "Packaging", "Migration and compatibility", "Contributor workflow"), not by 224 the order requirements were discussed. R-IDs stay continuous across groups 225 (R1, R2 in the first group; R3, R4 in the second; never restart at R1 per 226 group). 227 228 ## Rendering 229 230 The format-specific references describe how to render these sections in each 231 output format: 232 233 - **Markdown rendering:** `references/markdown-rendering.md` 234 - **HTML rendering:** `references/html-rendering.md` 235 236 This reference (`plan-sections.md`) is about WHAT the plan contains; 237 rendering references are about HOW each format presents it. The plan is 238 written in one format — markdown OR HTML, never both — based on the 239 resolved output mode. The section catalog is the same regardless of 240 format. 241

lastPrompt: I dont want to have to specify targets, and I dont want to have to specify .agents, the .agents should be the default for local-dir, and targets should be discovered by the CLI, which should be easy e…

content.text:
**Scoping synthesis** (Standard-depth plan — confirming scope before I spend research effort):

Based on your request, here's the scope I'm proposing to plan against:

Make `agent-sync init` work with zero flags: `--local-dir` defaults to `.agents` when no other source is given, and targets are auto-discovered from tool footprints instead of being required. Explicit `--target` and `--local-dir` values keep working as overrides. The interactive wizard picks up the same defaults (discovered targets pre-selected). No changes to `sync`/`validate`/`update` semantics or to any adapter.

**Call outs:**
- **Discovery timing:** snapshot discovered targets into `.agent-sync.yaml` at init (deterministic, my lean) vs. a persistent "auto" targets mode re-resolved on every sync (picks up newly installed tools, but manifest behavior varies by machine).
- **Discovery signals:** workspace tool dirs only (`.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.codex/`, `.agent/`) vs. also user-level config dirs and binaries on PATH (broader catch, more false positives).
- **Zero-match fallback:** if nothing is detected — fail with a hint, default to all bundled targets, or prompt interactively.
- **Bare `agent-sync init` on a terminal:** currently launches the wizard; keep that (wizard just gets smarter defaults) vs. make bare `init` non-interactive now that defaults exist.

Confirm and I'll proceed to research, drawing on this scope. (You can also redirect to /ce-brainstorm if this is bigger than you initially thought — I'll stop here and load it for you.)
Scope confirmed. Moving to research — dispatching the repo-research and learnings agents in parallel.

input.description: Repo research for init ergonomics
input.prompt:
Scope: technology, architecture, patterns in the repo /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync (a Go CLI called agent-sync that syncs AI-agent configuration from a canonical source to tool-native files for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Pi, Antigravity).

Planning context: We are planning a feature that improves `agent-sync init` ergonomics: (1) `--local-dir` should default to `.agents` when no other source flags are given; (2) `--target` should no longer be required — targets get auto-discovered from workspace tool footprints (e.g. .claude/, .cursor/, .codex/, .agent/ directories), snapshotted into the `.agent-sync.yaml` manifest at init time; explicit --target stays as an override; the interactive wizard keeps working but gets discovery-informed defaults. Open sub-decisions: which discovery signals (workspace dirs only vs also user-level config dirs / binaries on PATH), zero-match fallback behavior, and whether bare `agent-sync init` on a TTY still launches the wizard.

Please research and report (with repo-relative file paths and specific symbol names):

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## Institutional Learnings Search Results

### Search Context
- **Feature/Task**: `agent-sync init` ergonomics — default `--local-dir` to `.agents`, auto-discover targets from workspace footprints (`.claude/`, `.cursor/`, `.codex/`, `.agent/`), snapshot discovered targets into `.agent-sync.yaml`, discovery-informed wizard defaults, flags as overrides.
- **Keywords Used**: init, wizard, interactive/TTY/non-interactive, discover/probe, target, local-dir, manifest, fsroot, capability, footprint
- **Files Scanned**: 3 solutions docs (full corpus of `docs/solutions/`), 5 project memory files, plus targeted greps of `docs/plans/`, `docs/handoffs/`, `docs/spec/`, `docs/threat-model.md`
- **Relevant Matches**: 5 (3 solutions + 1 memory + 1 cluster of plan-level precedents)

### Critical Patterns
No `docs/solutions/patterns/critical-patterns.md` exists in this repo.

### Relevant Learnings

#### 1. Go cross-platform CI pitfalls — filepath, build tags, separator comparisons
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/solutions/best-practices/go-windows-cross-platform-pitfalls-2026-04-24.md`
- **Module**: `internal/fsroot`, `internal/cache`, `internal/workspace`
- **Problem Type**: `best_practice`
- **Severity**: high
- **Relevance**: Footprint discovery is exactly the kind of code this learning covers — filesystem probing and path-string handling that will hit the Windows CI matrix. The repo already got burned three ways in the original `internal/workspace` discovery code.
- **Key Insight**: (1) Never compare OS-joined paths against forward-slash constants — normalize with `filepath.ToSlash` first. (2) `filepath.IsAbs` alone is insufficient; reject leading `/` or `\` explicitly. (3) Platform-specific test helpers (symlinks, FIFOs) go in `//go:build !windows` files, never behind runtime `t.Skip`. Run `GOOS=windows go vet ./...` locally before pushing — the first PR to touch a new probe surface tends to surface every prior unit's latent Windows drift.

#### 2. Reconciling spec-vs-impl drift caught at PR review
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/solutions/workflow-issues/spec-impl-drift-at-pr-review-2026-04-25.md`
- **Module**: `internal/ir` (pattern is repo-wide)
- **Problem Type**: `workflow_issue`
- **Severity**: medium
- **Relevance**: Snapshotting discovered targets into the manifest is manifest schema evolution. `docs/spec/manifest-v1.md` exists and is prose, not generated — any new field (e.g., a discovered-targets snapshot, a `local_dir` default) will drift from the impl unless updated in the same PR.
- **Key Insight**: Update `docs/spec/manifest-v1.md` in the same commit/PR as the schema change (the `AGENTS.md` invariant "silent drift from the plan is a bug" operationalized). When auto-reviewers (gemini/copilot/codex run on every push here) flag spec-vs-impl mismatches with conflicting fixes, identify which side carries mechanical invariants before touching either — don't vote-count. Also: don't `t.Skip()` invariant tests; prefer spec-locked fixture tests.

#### 3. Safely renaming/introducing load-bearing on-disk identifiers — the substring-prefix trap
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/solutions/best-practices/large-mechanical-rename-of-load-bearing-identifiers.md`
- **Module**: repo-wide
- **Problem Type**: `best_practice`
- **Severity**: high
- **Relevance**: Adjacent but concretely applicable — this feature introduces new load-bearing on-disk identifiers (the `.agents` local-dir default, the footprint dir list, manifest snapshot field names). The doc's headline trap is a **direct hit for this plan**: `.aienv` vs `.aienvs` was the original bite, and your footprint list contains `.agent/` while the local-dir default is `.agents` — the exact same one-character substring-prefix pair. Any grep, glob, or matcher that probes for `.agent` will also match `.agents` (and `.agent-sync.yaml`) unless anchored.
- **Key Insight**: When one token is a prefix of another that must not match, write the discrimination rule explicitly (match `.agent` only when not followed by `s` or `-`), decide identifier spellings by context up front, and grep both the old form *and* the broken new form when verifying. On-disk names in the manifest are contracts — pick them deliberately and document them in the spec.

#### 4. Capability-lied gate is scope-aware — discovered targets can fail sync with zero-op shapes
- **File**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/.claude/projects/-Users-justinbeaudry-Projects-agent-sync/memory/capability-lie-gate-scope.md` (project memory, 2026-07-01; verify against current `internal/adapter/runtime.go` before relying on line numbers)
- **Module**: `internal/adapter` runtime + bundled adapters
- **Problem Type**: `runtime_error` / adapter-contract learning (inferred — memory file, no `problem_type`)
- **Relevance**: Auto-discovering targets means init will snapshot targets the user never explicitly chose. The runtime fails any session where a "supported" kind has an in-target IR node but the adapter emits zero non-warning ops (`ErrAdapterCapabilityLied`). A footprint-discovered target whose adapter skips kinds (by scope or otherwise) can turn a previously-clean `sync` into a hard failure — the Cursor adapter hit exactly this at user scope (PR #31).
- **Key Insight**: Footprints are scope-dependent (`.claude/` exists at both `~/.claude` and project scope; Cursor has no user-global home for rules). Discovery should be explicit about which scope it probed, and the plan should include tests for the zero-op shape per discovered target — a manifest containing only a kind the discovered adapter skips — not just the happy mixed case. The v0.5.0 zero-emit hint (#37) is the existing UX for "target emitted nothing"; discovery snapshotting should compose with it, not fight it.

#### 5. Existing init/discovery architectural precedents (plan-level KTDs, not solutions docs)
- **Files**: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/plans/2026-06-08-007-feat-cli-tui-sync-engine-plan.md` (KTD-4, KTD-5), `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/plans/2026-06-17-001-feat-hierarchy-discovery-plan.md`, `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/docs/threat-model.md`
- **Module**: `internal/cli`, `internal/hierarchy`
- **Problem Type**: `architecture_pattern` (inferred — these are accepted plan decisions, functionally institutional conventions)
- **Relevance**: The init command's shape was decided with explicit key technical decisions that the new feature must slot into rather than bypass.
- **Key Insight**:
  - **KTD-4**: wizard screens populate an `InitConfig` struct that the non-interactive flag path *also* populates — discovery should be a third populator of `InitConfig` defaults (flags > discovery > wizard/fail-fast), keeping wizard/flag parity and letting manifest-write logic stay TTY-free and testable.
  - **KTD-5**: TTY/non-interactive gating is centralized in `internal/cli/access`; non-interactive + missing required value = fail fast naming the exact flag. Decide explicitly what happens non-interactively when discovery finds **zero** footprints: today that's "fail naming `--target`" — discovery changes the required-value calculus and the plan should state the new rule.
  - **Hierarchy discovery precedent**: `internal/hierarchy` is a pure leaf package — read-only probes, no fsroot, no engine/adapter deps, table-driven temp-dir tests. Footprint discovery should mirror this shape. The `fsroot` invariant (CLAUDE.md) governs *writes* via `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot`; read-only stats of `.claude/` etc. have precedent outside fsroot, but keep probes strictly read-only.
  - **Threat model**: `init` pins refs to SHA (invariant #4); trust acceptance is interactive-only, CI never auto-accepts. Discovery-informed defaults must not soften any non-interactive trust or fail-fast behavior.

### Recommendations
- Update `docs/spec/manifest-v1.md` in the same PR as the manifest snapshot field; expect the three auto-reviewers to flag any drift, and resolve toward the side with mechanical invariants (learning #2).
- Anchor every `.agent` footprint match so it cannot match `.agents` or `.agent-sync.yaml` — this is the repo's documented substring-prefix trap replayed verbatim (learning #3).
- Route discovery through a read-only, dependency-free package modeled on `internal/hierarchy/discover.go`, feeding `InitConfig` with precedence flags > discovery > wizard prompt / non-interactive fail-fast; state the zero-footprints non-interactive behavior explicitly in the plan (learning #5).
- Add per-discovered-target zero-op tests and consider scope when snapshotting targets — a footprint's existence doesn't mean the adapter supports every kind at every scope, and the capability-lied gate turns that mismatch into a sync failure (learning #4).
- Normalize path comparisons with `filepath.ToSlash`, avoid runtime skips for platform-specific test fixtures, and run `GOOS=windows go vet ./...` before pushing the discovery code (learning #1). Note the darwin/amd64 CI job routinely stalls on runner scarcity — don't fix-loop on it (memory: `ci-darwin-amd64-runner-stall.md`).

No learnings exist specifically about the wizard's interaction design or manifest migration/versioning mechanics — if this feature surfaces reusable lessons there (e.g., how discovery-informed defaults interact with wizard preselection), they're worth capturing with `/ce-compound` after landing.</result>

<result>## Repository Research Summary

### Technology & Infrastructure

- **Language:** Go 1.25 (`go.mod`: `go 1.25`; AGENTS.md style section says "Go 1.25+. Use `os.Root` whenever touching user paths").
- **CLI framework:** `spf13/cobra` v1.9.1, wrapped by `charmbracelet/fang` v0.4.3 (root command construction in `internal/cli/root.go`, entrypoint `cmd/agent-sync/`).
- **TUI:** `charmbracelet/bubbletea` v1.3.10 + `bubbles` (textinput) + `lipgloss`. TTY detection via `mattn/go-isatty`.
- **YAML:** `goccy/go-yaml` (strict decode, AST-preserving rewrites); TOML via `pelletier/go-toml/v2`; git via `go-git/v5` plus shelling out (`internal/git`).
- **Deployment model:** single static binary CLI, goreleaser (`.goreleaser.yaml`), no server/API/DB. Bundled adapters run in-process; external adapters run as subprocesses over a JSON wire protocol (`internal/adapter/contract`).
- **Test frameworks:** stdlib `testing` only — no testify, no testscript, no golden-file harness in the CLI layer (the only `golden` dep is an indirect charmbracelet module). Table-driven tests + `t.TempDir()` + real filesystem. `go test -race ./...` and `golangci-lint run` are the gate (`AGENTS.md` PR checklist, `.golangci.yml`).
- **Module organization:** `internal/{cli, tui/wizard, manifest, workspace, hierarchy, adapter, adapter/bundled/{claude,cursor,codex,pi,antigravity}, engine, fsroot, ir, sync, trust, cache, git, worktree, ledger, locks, report, capmatrix, coverage, hooks, watch}`, plus public `pkg/adapterkit` (adapter SDK) and `conformance/echo`.

### Architecture & Structure

**1. `init` command implementation**

- File: `internal/cli/cmd_init.go`, `newInitCommand(deps RootDeps)`.
- Flags (all local to init, lines 128–135): `--source`, `--local-path`, `--local-dir`, `--ref`, `--commit`, `--floating`, `--target` (a `StringArrayVar`, repeatable, default `nil`), `--dir`. Global persistent flags in `internal/cli/root.go` lines 132–137: `--workspace`, `--output`, `--log-level`, `--offline`, `--non-interactive` (plus a `--yes` alias handled around root.go:169).
- Mode decision (cmd_init.go:65–87): `interactive := tui.Interactive(rc.Access.IsTTY, rc.Access.NonInteractive, rc.Access.Accessible)` — defined in `internal/tui/program.go:23` as `isTTY && !nonInteractive && !accessible`. The wizard runs **only if** `interactive && source == "" && localPath == "" && localDir == ""`. Note the current gate ignores `--target`: passing only `--target` on a TTY still launches the wizard; passing any source flag skips it. Otherwise it calls `requireFlag(rc.Access.NonInteractive, source!=""||localPath!=""||localDir!="", "--source/--local-path/--local-dir", ...)`.
- `requireFlag` / `MissingFlagError` live in `internal/cli/nonint.go` — error text `"required flag %s is missing in non-interactive mode (%s)"`, exit code `exitUsage`, explicitly tied to AGENTS invariant #3 (non-interactive never prompts, never hangs). This is the sanctioned pattern for any new required-value fail-fast.
- TTY/access resolution is centralized in `internal/cli/access.go` (`Access`, `resolveAccess`, `ResolveAccess`) — comment says "TTY/NO_COLOR/output-format detection lives here and nowhere else (KTD-5)". `NonInteractive` = flag OR stdin-not-a-TTY; `IsTTY` = stdin AND stdout are terminals (real `isatty(3)`, so `/dev/null` is not a TTY). Discovery-informed wizard defaults must not add a second detection path.
- Post-mode flow: `resolvePin` (cmd_init.go:146) pins ref→SHA (invariant #4; `--offline` + URL → error `"cannot resolve a remote ref under --offline; pass --commit to pin or --floating to skip pinning"`); `cfg.Validate()`; refuse-to-overwrite check via plain `os.Stat` on `manifestPathFor(cfg.Dir)`; `cfg.ManifestYAML()`; `manifest.WriteFile`; then a **reload check** — the written manifest is re-loaded with `manifest.LoadFile` and removed if it doesn't parse ("wrote an invalid manifest (removed)").
- **No validation of `--target` names at init.** Targets are written verbatim into the manifest; unknown names surface only at sync time as `engine.ErrAdapterNotFound` (`internal/engine/engine.go:19–21`). A discovery feature that snapshots targets would be the first place init cares about the adapter name set beyond wizard display.

**2. Interactive wizard**

- Package `internal/tui/wizard`: `init.go` (Bubble Tea model) + `initconfig.go` (`InitConfig` + validation + YAML rendering).
- `wizard.Run(ctx, in, out, noColor, availableTargets)` (init.go:31) returns `(InitConfig, committed bool, err)`. It renders to stderr (`deps.err()`), deliberately does **no git/network I/O** — pin resolution happens in the caller after the wizard returns. Discovery probing would fit the same pattern: compute before `wizard.Run` and pass results in.
- Linear phase machine: `phaseSource → phaseRef → phaseTargets → phaseConfirm → phaseDone`. Target defaults today: `newInitModel` (init.go:71) sorts `availableTargets` and marks **every target `selected: true`** — so "discovery-informed defaults" means changing which `targetChoice.selected` start true. The caller supplies the list via `bundledTargetNames()` (cmd_init.go:198), which reads `b.Manifest.Name` from `bundledAdapters()`.
- Source prompt has no default (placeholder text only); `looksLikeLocalPath` (init.go:250) heuristically classifies input as URL vs local path — there is currently **no wizard path that produces `LocalDir`**; only the flag path can set it. A `--local-dir` default of `.agents` would also be the first LocalDir entry point for the wizard.
- `InitConfig` (initconfig.go:16) is the documented single convergence point ("KTD-4: both the interactive wizard and the CLI's non-interactive flag path populate the same struct... flag parity is structural"). Any new discovery output (auto-selected targets, defaulted local-dir) should land in `InitConfig`, not in parallel plumbing.
- `InitConfig.Validate()` (initconfig.go:46): exactly one of SourceURL/LocalPath/LocalDir; LocalDir forbids ref/commit/floating; non-floating requires a commit; floating+local_path rejected. `ManifestYAML()` (initconfig.go:88) hand-renders YAML: `version: 1`, `canonical:` block, `trusted_sha` mirroring the pin, and a `targets:` list only when non-empty.

**3. Manifest schema and writing**

- Schema: `internal/manifest/schema.go` — `Manifest{Version, Canonical CanonicalSource, Targets []string, Scope string, Cache, Adapters []AdapterDecl, TrustedSHA, Trust, Compose}`. Strict decode: unknown keys rejected except `x-` prefixed (load.go). `Targets` is a plain `[]string`; **no name-grammar or registry validation at load** (nothing in `load.go` touches Targets). Spec: `docs/spec/manifest-v1.md` §`targets` — "Default (empty) means 'no adapters active' — a valid state for a workspace that is not yet configured." A discovery feature that snapshots targets at init changes that empty-is-valid ergonomic story but not the schema.
- `Scope` field already exists in the schema (`user | project | global`, "informs the adapters where the emitted files live"), separate from the runtime scope passed to `prepareScope`.
- Writing: `internal/manifest/write.go` — `WriteFile(path, content)` opens the **parent dir** via `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot` and uses `root.StagedWrite` (atomic, crash-safe, 0o644). `WriteResolvedSHA`/`WriteTrustedSHA` do AST-preserving in-place key updates and require the key to pre-exist (`ErrKeyMissing`) — relevant if discovery ever wants to *update* `targets:` in an existing manifest rather than write a fresh one.
- Manifest filename constant: `workspace.ManifestName` (`.agent-sync.yaml`) in `internal/workspace/workspace.go`.

**4. Adapter/target registry and native-directory metadata**

- Canonical bundled list: `internal/cli/setup.go:23` `bundledAdapters()` → `[]*adapter.BundledAdapter{claude, cursor, codex, pi, antigravity}` ("Centralized so every command discovers the same adapters"). Wizard names come from `bundledTargetNames()`.
- **Tool-footprint metadata already exists in two forms:**
  - `AdapterManifest.ReservedPrefix` (`internal/adapter/manifest.go:67`) — the workspace-relative directory each adapter owns. Constants per adapter: claude `".claude"` (`internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go:36`), cursor `".cursor"`, codex `".codex"`, pi `".pi"`, antigravity `".agent"` (each in its `bundled.go`). This is exactly the workspace-dir signal set the planning context lists, and it is already reachable from `bundledAdapters()[i].Manifest.ReservedPrefix` — discovery can map "`.cursor/` exists → enable `cursor`" without any new metadata table. Validated by `manifest.ValidateReservedPrefix` (wrapped in `adapter.ValidateReservedPrefix`).
  - Scope-aware `pathSet`/`resolvePathSet(scope)` per adapter (e.g. `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go:41` — user scope: `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `.claude.json`; `internal/adapter/bundled/cursor/emit_tool_owned.go:44`). These are **package-private and emit-oriented**, not exported metadata — a user-scope discovery signal (e.g. `~/.claude` exists) would need either new exported metadata on `BundledAdapter` or a discovery table in the CLI layer. There is no exported "footprint" API today.
- Runtime registry: `adapter.DiscoverAdapters(ctx, adapter.DiscoverOptions{Workspace: m, Bundled: bundledAdapters()})` (`internal/adapter/discover.go`) also finds PATH adapters named `agent-sync-adapter-<name>` — note that out-of-tree adapters exist, so "bundled names" is not necessarily the full valid-target universe.

**5. Scope handling (project vs user)**

- `sync` has `--user` (`internal/cli/cmd_sync.go:127`, `"also sync the user-level (~) manifest"`). Plain sync uses hierarchy discovery (`internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go`, `hierarchySyncOptions{IncludeUser: userScope}`); explicit `--workspace` pins single project scope and makes `--user` an error (cmd_sync.go:74–80).
- Scope discovery: `internal/hierarchy/discover.go` — `Discover(cwd, Options{Home, IncludeUser, ...})` returns ordered `Scope{Root, ManifestPath, Level (user/project/directory), Emit}`. Project root = nearest `.git` ancestor (`findProjectRoot`); user scope = manifest at `$HOME` (`userScope`/`UserScope`), Emit only under `--user`. `Options.Home` is injectable for tests.
- `prepareScope(ctx, rc, scopeRoot, manifestPath, scope, now)` (`internal/cli/setup.go:90`) threads the scope string down to adapters via `engine.Request.Scope` → `initialize` params → `resolvePathSet(scope)`.
- **`init` itself has no scope concept today** — no `--user` on init; it writes wherever `--dir`/`--workspace` points (including `~` if you point it there). If discovery differs per scope, init currently has no existing flag to hang that on.

**6. fsroot constraints for filesystem probing**

- `internal/fsroot` (`root.go`, `containment.go`, `safewrite.go`) is the **write** enforcement point. AGENTS.md invariant #1: "All writes into a workspace go through `internal/fsroot`"; the project CLAUDE.md restates it as "Do not reach outside `internal/fsroot` to touch user paths" with the note that every scope-root write goes through `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot`.
- **Read-only probes are established precedent outside fsroot:** `cmd_init.go` uses bare `os.Stat` for the already-exists check; `internal/hierarchy/discover.go` uses `os.Stat`/`os.Lstat` for `manifestAt`/`hasGit`; `internal/workspace/discover.go` walks parents with `os.Lstat`/`os.Stat` (with careful symlink handling — see `validateManifestFile`). So probing for `.claude/`, `.cursor/`, etc. during init with `os.Stat` on the destination dir is consistent with existing patterns; only the manifest **write** must stay on `manifest.WriteFile` → `fsroot.StagedWrite`. If discovery probes user-home dirs, follow the hierarchy package's injectable-`Home` pattern for testability.
- Style note (AGENTS.md): "Use `os.Root` whenever touching user paths" — if probing grows beyond a couple of stats (e.g. reading inside `~`), prefer opening a root.

### Implementation Patterns

**7. Test conventions**

- Init CLI tests: `internal/cli/cmd_init_test.go` — helper `runInit(t, args...)` constructs a full root command in-process (`NewRootCommand(RootDeps{Out, Err, Version:"test"})`, `root.SetArgs(append([]string{"init","--non-interactive"}, args...))`, `root.Execute()`) and asserts by re-loading the written manifest with `manifest.LoadFile`. Behavior-named tests (`TestInit_NonInteractiveLocalDirWritesUnpinnedManifest`, `TestInit_RefusesToOverwrite`, `TestInit_ThenSync` — a real init→sync integration in one process). Git-needing tests gate on `requireGit(t)` / `makeCanonicalRepo(t)` from `internal/cli/cmd_sync_test.go:16,28`. Pin resolution has its own file `cmd_init_resolvepin_test.go`.
- Wizard tests: `internal/tui/wizard/wizard_test.go` — table-driven `TestInitConfig_Validate` / `TestInitConfig_ManifestYAML`, plus **model-level flow tests** that drive the Bubble Tea model directly with `tea.KeyMsg`s (`TestInitModel_FullFlowProducesConfig`, `TestInitModel_EscAborts`) — no PTY, no snapshot/golden files.
- No testscript or golden-file harness anywhere in cli/tui. Adapter conformance corpus lives in `internal/adapter/conformance` (JSON corpus, sorted case loading) and `conformance/echo` — not relevant to init. Standard `testing` + plain `t.Fatalf`; `t.TempDir()` everywhere; access/TTY logic tested via the pure `resolveAccess(accessInput)` seam (`internal/cli/access_test.go`).

**8. AGENTS.md / docs constraints that bind this feature**

- `AGENTS.md` invariant #3: non-interactive mode never prompts, never hangs — exits with a documented code and **the exact flag needed to proceed** (that's `MissingFlagError`). If `--target` stops being required, the non-interactive path must still be deterministic; if discovery finds zero targets, silently writing `targets: []` is schema-valid (spec explicitly blesses empty), so the open "zero-match fallback" decision has a spec-sanctioned floor.
- Invariant #4: pinning is default; `local_dir` is the documented unpinned exception (and the `.agents` example is named right in the invariant — supporting `--local-dir` defaulting to `.agents`). Note the reserved `agent-sync-` skill-id prefix in `.agents/skills`.
- Invariant #1: writes through fsroot only (see §6); the home root is opened only on explicit `--user` — a discovery pass that *reads* user-home tool dirs during project init would be new territory; the closest precedent is hierarchy composition's "read-only user-root open" carve-out documented in invariant #1.
- Plan-first rule: "If code disagrees with the plan, update the plan first in the same PR." Project `CLAUDE.md` repeats this and requires `go test -race ./...` + `golangci-lint run` before done.
- Master plan Unit 17 (`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md` lines ~1178–1210) specifies the full wizard branching-state spec (fresh/existing manifest, ancestor detected, remote unreachable, zero targets, pre-existing target outputs) and an authoritative `docs/wizard/init-states.md` — **which does not exist yet** (no `docs/wizard/` dir), and the shipped wizard is the simpler linear model (init.go comment: "the nav stack is reserved for the deeper branching the master plan enumerates"). This feature lands squarely in Unit 17's unfinished territory; expect to touch the plan.

**9. STRATEGY.md** — does **not** exist at the repo root (nor anywhere in the repo; a case-insensitive search found nothing). The strategic docs are `docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-aienvs-agent-workspace-requirements.md`, the master plan above, and `docs/spec/*` (notably `manifest-v1.md`).

**10. Recent related work (docs/plans + CHANGELOG)**

- Plan naming: `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-NNN-<type>-<slug>-plan.md`, conventional-commit-typed (`feat`/`fix`/`refactor`/`test`); the master plan is organized into checkbox **Units** (Unit 16 = cobra command tree + non-interactive fail-fast; Unit 17 = init/target wizards — both still `[ ]` unchecked).
- Most relevant plans: `2026-06-16-001-feat-in-repo-local-skill-source-plan.md` (introduced `--local-dir`/`canonical.local_dir`), `2026-06-17-001-feat-hierarchy-discovery-plan.md` + `2026-06-17-002-feat-multi-scope-sync-plan.md` (scope discovery, `--user`), `2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md` (the read-only user-root precedent, D8).
- CHANGELOG (v0.7.0 area): the **zero-emit hint** — plain `sync` with no project manifest now emits an advisory pointing at `agent-sync sync --user` or `agent-sync init` (additive `notice` field in JSON, `nothing to sync: …` in text). That hint is the funnel into init that this ergonomics feature improves. Also: `--local-dir .agents` configuration is documented in the 0.4.x entry ("Configure with `agent-sync init --local-dir .agents`"), and adapters were made scope-aware across 0.5–0.7 (claude/cursor/codex user-scope paths, pi, antigravity with reserved prefix `.agent`). Nothing in the CHANGELOG yet touches target auto-discovery or init defaults.

**Key symbols for the implementers, in one place:** `newInitCommand`, `resolvePin`, `manifestPathFor`, `bundledTargetNames` (internal/cli/cmd_init.go); `requireFlag`/`MissingFlagError` (internal/cli/nonint.go); `resolveAccess`/`Access` (internal/cli/access.go); `tui.Interactive` (internal/tui/program.go); `wizard.Run`, `InitConfig`, `newInitModel`, `targetChoice` (internal/tui/wizard); `manifest.Manifest`, `manifest.WriteFile` (internal/manifest); `bundledAdapters` (internal/cli/setup.go); `AdapterManifest.ReservedPrefix` + per-adapter `reservedPrefix` constants (internal/adapter/bundled/*/bundled.go); `hierarchy.Discover`, `UserScope`, `Options.Home` (internal/hierarchy); `workspace.Find`, `workspace.ManifestName` (internal/workspace); `engine.ErrAdapterNotFound` (internal/engine/engine.go).</result>

- Writing: `internal/manifest/write.go` — `WriteFile(path, content)` opens the **parent dir** via `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot` and uses `root.StagedWrite` (atomic, crash-safe, 0o644). `WriteResolvedSHA`/`WriteTrustedSHA` do AST-preserving in-place key updates and require the key to pre-exist (`ErrKeyMissing`) — relevant if discovery ever wants to *update* `targets:` in an existing manifest rather than write a fresh one.
- Manifest filename constant: `workspace.ManifestName` (`.agent-sync.yaml`) in `internal/workspace/workspace.go`.

**4. Adapter/target registry and native-directory metadata**

- Canonical bundled list: `internal/cli/setup.go:23` `bundledAdapters()` → `[]*adapter.BundledAdapter{claude, cursor, codex, pi, antigravity}` ("Centralized so every command discovers the same adapters"). Wizard names come from `bundledTargetNames()`.
- **Tool-footprint metadata already exists in two forms:**
  - `AdapterManifest.ReservedPrefix` (`internal/adapter/manifest.go:67`) — the workspace-relative directory each adapter owns. Constants per adapter: claude `".claude"` (`internal/adapter/bundled/claude/bundled.go:36`), cursor `".cursor"`, codex `".codex"`, pi `".pi"`, antigravity `".agent"` (each in its `bundled.go`). This is exactly the workspace-dir signal set the planning context lists, and it is already reachable from `bundledAdapters()[i].Manifest.ReservedPrefix` — discovery can map "`.cursor/` exists → enable `cursor`" without any new metadata table. Validated by `manifest.ValidateReservedPrefix` (wrapped in `adapter.ValidateReservedPrefix`).
  - Scope-aware `pathSet`/`resolvePathSet(scope)` per adapter (e.g. `internal/adapter/bundled/claude/emit_tool_owned.go:41` — user scope: `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `.claude.json`; `internal/adapter/bundled/cursor/emit_tool_owned.go:44`). These are **package-private and emit-oriented**, not exported metadata — a user-scope discovery signal (e.g. `~/.claude` exists) would need either new exported metadata on `BundledAdapter` or a discovery table in the CLI layer. There is no exported "footprint" API today.
- Runtime registry: `adapter.DiscoverAdapters(ctx, adapter.DiscoverOptions{Workspace: m, Bundled: bundledAdapters()})` (`internal/adapter/discover.go`) also finds PATH adapters named `agent-sync-adapter-<name>` — note that out-of-tree adapters exist, so "bundled names" is not necessarily the full valid-target universe.

**5. Scope handling (project vs user)**

- `sync` has `--user` (`internal/cli/cmd_sync.go:127`, `"also sync the user-level (~) manifest"`). Plain sync uses hierarchy discovery (`internal/cli/hierarchy_sync.go`, `hierarchySyncOptions{IncludeUser: userScope}`); explicit `--workspace` pins single project scope and makes `--user` an error (cmd_sync.go:74–80).
- Scope discovery: `internal/hierarchy/discover.go` — `Discover(cwd, Options{Home, IncludeUser, ...})` returns ordered `Scope{Root, ManifestPath, Level (user/project/directory), Emit}`. Project root = nearest `.git` ancestor (`findProjectRoot`); user scope = manifest at `$HOME` (`userScope`/`UserScope`), Emit only under `--user`. `Options.Home` is injectable for tests.
- `prepareScope(ctx, rc, scopeRoot, manifestPath, scope, now)` (`internal/cli/setup.go:90`) threads the scope string down to adapters via `engine.Request.Scope` → `initialize` params → `resolvePathSet(scope)`.
- **`init` itself has no scope concept today** — no `--user` on init; it writes wherever `--dir`/`--workspace` points (including `~` if you point it there). If discovery differs per scope, init currently has no existing flag to hang that on.

**6. fsroot constraints for filesystem probing**

- `internal/fsroot` (`root.go`, `containment.go`, `safewrite.go`) is the **write** enforcement point. AGENTS.md invariant #1: "All writes into a workspace go through `internal/fsroot`"; the project CLAUDE.md restates it as "Do not reach outside `internal/fsroot` to touch user paths" with the note that every scope-root write goes through `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot`.
- **Read-only probes are established precedent outside fsroot:** `cmd_init.go` uses bare `os.Stat` for the already-exists check; `internal/hierarchy/discover.go` uses `os.Stat`/`os.Lstat` for `manifestAt`/`hasGit`; `internal/workspace/discover.go` walks parents with `os.Lstat`/`os.Stat` (with careful symlink handling — see `validateManifestFile`). So probing for `.claude/`, `.cursor/`, etc. during init with `os.Stat` on the destination dir is consistent with existing patterns; only the manifest **write** must stay on `manifest.WriteFile` → `fsroot.StagedWrite`. If discovery probes user-home dirs, follow the hierarchy package's injectable-`Home` pattern for testability.
- Style note (AGENTS.md): "Use `os.Root` whenever touching user paths" — if probing grows beyond a couple of stats (e.g. reading inside `~`), prefer opening a root.

### Implementation Patterns

**7. Test conventions**

- Init CLI tests: `internal/cli/cmd_init_test.go` — helper `runInit(t, args...)` constructs a full root command in-process (`NewRootCommand(RootDeps{Out, Err, Version:"test"})`, `root.SetArgs(append([]string{"init","--non-interactive"}, args...))`, `root.Execute()`) and asserts by re-loading the written manifest with `manifest.LoadFile`. Behavior-named tests (`TestInit_NonInteractiveLocalDirWritesUnpinnedManifest`, `TestInit_RefusesToOverwrite`, `TestInit_ThenSync` — a real init→sync integration in one process). Git-needing tests gate on `requireGit(t)` / `makeCanonicalRepo(t)` from `internal/cli/cmd_sync_test.go:16,28`. Pin resolution has its own file `cmd_init_resolvepin_test.go`.
- Wizard tests: `internal/tui/wizard/wizard_test.go` — table-driven `TestInitConfig_Validate` / `TestInitConfig_ManifestYAML`, plus **model-level flow tests** that drive the Bubble Tea model directly with `tea.KeyMsg`s (`TestInitModel_FullFlowProducesConfig`, `TestInitModel_EscAborts`) — no PTY, no snapshot/golden files.
- No testscript or golden-file harness anywhere in cli/tui. Adapter conformance corpus lives in `internal/adapter/conformance` (JSON corpus, sorted case loading) and `conformance/echo` — not relevant to init. Standard `testing` + plain `t.Fatalf`; `t.TempDir()` everywhere; access/TTY logic tested via the pure `resolveAccess(accessInput)` seam (`internal/cli/access_test.go`).

**8. AGENTS.md / docs constraints that bind this feature**

- `AGENTS.md` invariant #3: non-interactive mode never prompts, never hangs — exits with a documented code and **the exact flag needed to proceed** (that's `MissingFlagError`). If `--target` stops being required, the non-interactive path must still be deterministic; if discovery finds zero targets, silently writing `targets: []` is schema-valid (spec explicitly blesses empty), so the open "zero-match fallback" decision has a spec-sanctioned floor.
- Invariant #4: pinning is default; `local_dir` is the documented unpinned exception (and the `.agents` example is named right in the invariant — supporting `--local-dir` defaulting to `.agents`). Note the reserved `agent-sync-` skill-id prefix in `.agents/skills`.
- Invariant #1: writes through fsroot only (see §6); the home root is opened only on explicit `--user` — a discovery pass that *reads* user-home tool dirs during project init would be new territory; the closest precedent is hierarchy composition's "read-only user-root open" carve-out documented in invariant #1.
- Plan-first rule: "If code disagrees with the plan, update the plan first in the same PR." Project `CLAUDE.md` repeats this and requires `go test -race ./...` + `golangci-lint run` before done.
- Master plan Unit 17 (`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md` lines ~1178–1210) specifies the full wizard branching-state spec (fresh/existing manifest, ancestor detected, remote unreachable, zero targets, pre-existing target outputs) and an authoritative `docs/wizard/init-states.md` — **which does not exist yet** (no `docs/wizard/` dir), and the shipped wizard is the simpler linear model (init.go comment: "the nav stack is reserved for the deeper branching the master plan enumerates"). This feature lands squarely in Unit 17's unfinished territory; expect to touch the plan.

**9. STRATEGY.md** — does **not** exist at the repo root (nor anywhere in the repo; a case-insensitive search found nothing). The strategic docs are `docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-aienvs-agent-workspace-requirements.md`, the master plan above, and `docs/spec/*` (notably `manifest-v1.md`).

**10. Recent related work (docs/plans + CHANGELOG)**

- Plan naming: `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-NNN-<type>-<slug>-plan.md`, conventional-commit-typed (`feat`/`fix`/`refactor`/`test`); the master plan is organized into checkbox **Units** (Unit 16 = cobra command tree + non-interactive fail-fast; Unit 17 = init/target wizards — both still `[ ]` unchecked).
- Most relevant plans: `2026-06-16-001-feat-in-repo-local-skill-source-plan.md` (introduced `--local-dir`/`canonical.local_dir`), `2026-06-17-001-feat-hierarchy-discovery-plan.md` + `2026-06-17-002-feat-multi-scope-sync-plan.md` (scope discovery, `--user`), `2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md` (the read-only user-root precedent, D8).
- CHANGELOG (v0.7.0 area): the **zero-emit hint** — plain `sync` with no project manifest now emits an advisory pointing at `agent-sync sync --user` or `agent-sync init` (additive `notice` field in JSON, `nothing to sync: …` in text). That hint is the funnel into init that this ergonomics feature improves. Also: `--local-dir .agents` configuration is documented in the 0.4.x entry ("Configure with `agent-sync init --local-dir .agents`"), and adapters were made scope-aware across 0.5–0.7 (claude/cursor/codex user-scope paths, pi, antigravity with reserved prefix `.agent`). Nothing in the CHANGELOG yet touches target auto-discovery or init defaults.

**Key symbols for the implementers, in one place:** `newInitCommand`, `resolvePin`, `manifestPathFor`, `bundledTargetNames` (internal/cli/cmd_init.go); `requireFlag`/`MissingFlagError` (internal/cli/nonint.go); `resolveAccess`/`Access` (internal/cli/access.go); `tui.Interactive` (internal/tui/program.go); `wizard.Run`, `InitConfig`, `newInitModel`, `targetChoice` (internal/tui/wizard); `manifest.Manifest`, `manifest.WriteFile` (internal/manifest); `bundledAdapters` (internal/cli/setup.go); `AdapterManifest.ReservedPrefix` + per-adapter `reservedPrefix` constants (internal/adapter/bundled/*/bundled.go); `hierarchy.Discover`, `UserScope`, `Options.Home` (internal/hierarchy); `workspace.Find`, `workspace.ManifestName` (internal/workspace); `engine.ErrAdapterNotFound` (internal/engine/engine.go).</result>

content.text:
Research is consolidated, and the picture is favorable: the adapters already expose exactly the footprint metadata discovery needs (`ReservedPrefix` — `.claude`, `.cursor`, `.codex`, `.pi`, `.agent`), and there's an established `InitConfig` convergence pattern (flags and wizard populate the same struct) that discovery slots into as a third populator. External research isn't needed — this is repo-internal CLI ergonomics with strong local patterns, so I'm skipping it. (Slack tools are available if you ever want organizational context folded in; not doing that unprompted.)

Since this is a Standard-depth plan with real flow complexity (interactive × non-interactive × source flags × discovery results × scope), I'm running the flow analyzer before structuring the plan.

input.description: Flow and edge-case analysis
input.prompt:
Analyze the user flows and edge cases for a planned feature in the Go CLI repo /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync.

Feature: `agent-sync init` ergonomics.
1. `--local-dir` defaults to `.agents` when NO source flag (`--source`/`--local-path`/`--local-dir`) is given — so bare `agent-sync init --non-interactive` succeeds, writing a manifest with `canonical.local_dir: .agents`.
2. `--target` is no longer required: targets are auto-discovered by probing the workspace dir for each bundled adapter's reserved-prefix directory (`.claude` → claude, `.cursor` → cursor, `.codex` → codex, `.pi` → pi, `.agent` → antigravity; exact os.Stat on dir names, workspace-relative). Discovered target names are snapshotted into the manifest's existing `targets:` list at init time (no schema change). Explicit `--target` flags override discovery entirely.
3. Interactive wizard (bare `init` on a TTY) keeps running but gains discovery-informed defaults: discovered targets pre-selected in the target screen (today ALL targets default selected); the source screen gains a `.agents` local-dir default.
4. Zero-match fallback (lean): non-interactive with no --target and zero footprints discovered → fail fast naming `--target` (MissingFlagError pattern); interactive → wizard target screen with nothing (or all?) preselected.

Current implementation facts (from repo research):
- `internal/cli/cmd_init.go` `newInitCommand`: wizard runs only if interactive AND no source flags given; `--target` alone on a TTY still launches the wizard. Non-interactive missing source → `requireFlag` fail-fast naming `--source/--local-path/--local-dir`.
- `internal/tui/wizard` `InitConfig` is the single convergence struct for flag path and wizard (KTD-4). Wizard does no git/network I/O; the wizard currently has NO path that produces LocalDir. `newInitModel` pre-selects all targets.
- Manifest `targets:` is []string, no validation at load; empty is spec-valid ("not yet configured"). Unknown target names error only at sync (`engine.ErrAdapterNotFound`).
- `init` has no `--user` flag; it writes wherever `--dir` points (could be ~). Footprints are workspace-relative, so probing `--dir` covers both project and home cases.
- init refuses to overwrite an existing `.agent-sync.yaml`.
- Out-of-tree adapters exist (`agent-sync-adapter-<name>` on PATH), so bundled names aren't the full valid-target universe.
- `--local-dir` manifests are unpinned (no commit/trust); the `.agents` dir need not exist for the manifest to be valid.
- AGENTS.md invariants: non-interactive never prompts/hangs, fails fast naming the exact flag; all writes via fsroot (reads may use os.Stat per precedent).

Enumerate: (a) all user flows through init after this change (flag combinations × interactive/non-interactive × discovery outcomes), (b) edge cases and state transitions we may have missed, (c) ambiguities the plan must resolve, (d) any handoff gaps with sync (e.g. discovered target whose adapter emits zero ops at some scope; `.agents` default when the dir doesn't exist; `--target` for a PATH adapter that discovery can't see). Keep it concrete and prioritized; flag which gaps are must-resolve vs nice-to-have.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Grounding complete. Key files read: `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/internal/cli/cmd_init.go`, `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/internal/cli/nonint.go`, `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/internal/cli/access.go`, `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/internal/tui/wizard/init.go`, `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/internal/tui/wizard/initconfig.go`, `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync/internal/worktree/reader.go`, plus bundled `capabilities.yaml` reserved prefixes and `internal/adapter/discover.go`. Findings below are grounded in those.

---

## (a) User flows after the change

Decision axes: source flags (none / `--source` / `--local-path` / `--local-dir`) × targets (none / `--target…`) × mode (interactive TTY / non-interactive / accessible-TTY) × discovery outcome (some / zero / stat-error). The mode gate is `tui.Interactive(IsTTY, NonInteractive, Accessible)` — note **accessible is a third state**: not interactive, but `requireFlag` no-ops because `Access.NonInteractive` stays false (`access.go:64`, `nonint.go:34`).

**Non-interactive (or piped stdin):**

1. Bare `init --non-interactive`, footprints found → LocalDir defaults to `.agents`, discovery snapshots targets, manifest written. New happy path.
2. Bare `init --non-interactive`, **zero** footprints → per spec item 4: `MissingFlagError` naming `--target`. **Directly contradicts item 1's promise** (see Gap 1).
3. `init --non-interactive --target claude` (no source) → `.agents` default + explicit targets, discovery skipped entirely. Succeeds.
4. `init --non-interactive --source URL` (no target) → discovery runs. Some found → snapshot; zero found → fail per item 4. **Today this succeeds with an empty (spec-valid) `targets:` list — breaking change** (see Gap 1).
5. `init --non-interactive --local-dir custom/` → explicit source, discovery still governs targets (same as 4).
6. `init --non-interactive --ref main` (no source) → default kicks in → `InitConfig.Validate` rejects ref+LocalDir with *"a local directory … cannot set a ref, commit, or --floating"* — confusing, since the user never asked for local_dir (Gap 2). Same for `--commit`, `--floating`.
7. `--offline` + bare init → fine (LocalDir short-circuits `resolvePin` at `cmd_init.go:148`). `--offline --source URL` → still fails pin resolution, unchanged.
8. Any flow where `.agent-sync.yaml` exists → refused (unchanged; check happens after wizard/discovery — pre-existing wart).

**Interactive TTY:**

9. Bare `init` → wizard, with discovered targets pre-selected and a `.agents` source default. Requires wizard rework (Gap 3).
10. Bare `init`, zero footprints → wizard target screen: nothing or all preselected? Spec leaves it open (Gap 8 recommendation below).
11. `init --target claude` on TTY (no source) → **today launches the wizard** (`cmd_init.go:66` gates only on source flags). With a source default, does it now go fully non-interactive with source=`.agents`? Unspecified (Gap 5).
12. `init --source URL` on TTY (no target) → wizard skipped today; discovery runs. Zero match → `requireFlag(nonInteractive=false, …)` returns nil → empty-targets manifest. So TTY and non-TTY diverge for identical flags — consistent with the existing source `requireFlag` precedent, but should be pinned in tests.

**Accessible (TERM=dumb / AGENT_SYNC_ACCESSIBLE on a TTY):**

13. Bare `init` accessible → no wizard, `NonInteractive=false` so no `MissingFlagError`. Today: falls through to `cfg.Validate()` and dies with the poor "wizard: set exactly one of…" error. After the change: silently writes the `.agents` + discovered-targets manifest. Zero-match → silent empty-targets manifest (requireFlag no-ops). Spec item 4 only branches interactive vs non-interactive; this third state needs a decided behavior.

**`--dir` / `--workspace`:**

14. `init --dir sub/ --non-interactive` → discovery must probe `destDir` (dir → workspace → cwd, `cmd_init.go:49-52`), not cwd. `--dir` pointing at a nonexistent directory → all probes fail → zero-match error *"pass --target"*, masking the real problem (bad `--dir`); the manifest write would also fail, but the user sees the wrong error first (Gap 6).
15. `init --dir ~` (home) → probes `~/.claude`, `~/.cursor`, `~/.codex`, `~/.pi`, `~/.agent`. Antigravity's **user-scope** footprint is `.gemini`, not `.agent` (`bundled/antigravity/capabilities.yaml`), so antigravity users are never discovered at home. Conversely `~/.cursor`/`~/.codex` exist for anyone who merely installed those tools — at home that's arguably the right signal, but the asymmetry is real (Gap 7).

---

## (b) Edge cases / state transitions

- **`.claude` is a file, not a dir** → plan says "os.Stat on dir names"; must add an `IsDir()` check or a file named `.pi` fires discovery.
- **Symlinked footprint dir** → `os.Stat` follows symlinks; a dangling symlink is not discovered. Fine, but state it.
- **Stat error other than NotExist** (permission-denied dir) → skip-silently vs fatal? Existing init precedent treats non-NotExist stat as fatal (`cmd_init.go:104-107`); for a 5-dir probe, fatal on one unreadable dir is harsh. Decide and document.
- **Case-insensitive APFS/NTFS**: `.CLAUDE` will satisfy `os.Stat(".claude")` on the default macOS filesystem. Accept as minor; don't try to fight it.
- **`.agents` vs `.agent`**: the source default and antigravity's footprint differ by one character. Users will typo `--local-dir .agent`; error copy and docs should keep these visually distinct.
- **Ordering**: discovered targets must be written sorted (wizard already sorts, `wizard/init.go:79`); a map-driven probe would otherwise produce nondeterministic `targets:` order — noisy diffs, flaky tests.
- **Duplicate `--target claude --target claude`** → duplicate manifest entries. Pre-existing; spec says explicit overrides discovery with no merge, so no new dedup path needed, but worth a normalizing pass while touching this.
- **Wizard with zero targets selected** → today Enter proceeds to confirm with empty `Targets` → valid empty-targets manifest. Fine; keep, but a confirm-screen "targets: (none)" render is needed (currently renders an empty join).
- **Existing-manifest check ordering**: today the user completes the whole wizard before hitting "already exists" (`cmd_init.go:101`). Adding discovery makes the pre-write work heavier; move the stat before wizard/discovery while in here.

---

## (c) Ambiguities the plan must resolve (prioritized)

**Must-resolve**

1. **Item 1 vs item 4 contradiction + breaking change.** In a fresh empty repo, bare `agent-sync init --non-interactive` has no source flags *and* zero footprints — item 1 says it succeeds, item 4 says it fails. Also, `init --non-interactive --source URL` with no `--target` succeeds *today* (empty `targets:` is explicitly spec-valid — `manifest/schema.go:13` "a valid state for a workspace that is not yet configured"), and item 4 would make it fail. **Recommended default:** write the empty-targets manifest with a stderr hint ("no targets discovered; add with `--target` or edit targets: in .agent-sync.yaml") rather than `MissingFlagError`. That preserves item 1, avoids the regression, and the existing zero-emit hint (#37) plus `engine.ErrAdapterNotFound` already backstop sync. If fail-fast is truly wanted, scope it to *bare* init only and say so.
2. **Pin flags × defaulted source.** `--ref`/`--commit`/`--floating` with no source flag must produce a purpose-built error ("--ref requires --source or --local-path; the default .agents source is unpinned"), not the generic LocalDir validation error from `initconfig.go:63-67`. Decide: error (recommended) vs suppress-the-default.
3. **Wizard LocalDir mechanics.** The wizard has *no* LocalDir path: `looksLikeLocalPath` would classify a typed `.agents` as **LocalPath**, sending it into `git.ResolveLocalRef` → failure. "Source screen gains a `.agents` default" needs concrete design: (a) empty-Enter accepts LocalDir=`.agents` (today empty Enter is rejected, `wizard/init.go:118-120`); (b) LocalDir selection must **skip phaseRef** (ref+local_dir is invalid); (c) `sourceDisplay()` and confirm screen must render local_dir; (d) how does a user type a *custom* local-dir vs a local git path — probably a source-type choice, not string heuristics. Also: discovery is fs I/O — do it in `cmd_init.go` and pass results into `wizard.Run` (new parameter), keeping the wizard I/O-free per KTD-4.
4. **`.agents` missing at sync.** Manifest is valid, but first `sync` hard-fails with `worktree.ErrSourceMissing` (`worktree/reader.go:43`). Bare init succeeding into a guaranteed sync failure is a bad handoff. **Recommend:** create `.agents/` at init when defaulted (via `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot` — the CLAUDE.md invariant; reads may os.Stat, writes may not) — an *empty* existing dir degrades to the soft zero-emit hint instead of a hard error. Alternative: print "create .agents/ before running sync". Pick one.
5. **Does `--target` alone on a TTY still launch the wizard?** (Flow 11.) Today yes. If the source default applies, the invocation becomes fully specified and should skip the wizard — but that's a silent behavior change for TTY users. Decide and test explicitly; the wizard-gate condition at `cmd_init.go:66` is the exact line in question.

**Important**

6. **Probe base + bad `--dir` error ordering.** Probe `destDir`; stat `destDir` itself first so a nonexistent `--dir` fails with "no such directory", not "pass --target".
7. **Home-dir semantics.** Either (a) declare discovery project-scope-only and document that `init --dir ~` may under-discover (antigravity's user footprint is `.gemini`), or (b) add per-scope footprints. Given init has no `--user` flag, (a) is the lean answer — but write it down.
8. **Zero-match wizard preselection.** Recommend: discovered set pre-selected when non-empty; **all** pre-selected when zero discovered (preserves today's greenfield UX and makes zero-match non-punishing). "None preselected" + Enter yields an empty-targets manifest one keystroke away — hostile default.
9. **Probe list source of truth.** Derive from `bundledAdapters()[i].Manifest.ReservedPrefix` (already validated, present on all five), not a hardcoded map — new bundled adapters then get discovery for free. Decide explicitly that PATH adapters (`internal/adapter/discover.go`, which carry `reserved_prefix` but require subprocess execution to read) do **not** participate in v1, and say why (no subprocess exec at init).
10. **Accessible mode.** Pin flow 13's behavior in tests: default + discovery apply, silent success is acceptable, zero-match follows whatever Gap 1 decides.
11. **Announce the magic.** Success output must name what was defaulted/discovered: `wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude, cursor [discovered])`. A defaulting feature that prints only `wrote .agent-sync.yaml` hides misdiscovery until sync.

**Nice-to-have**

12. Warn (don't fail) at init when an explicit `--target` name is neither bundled nor `agent-sync-adapter-<name>` on PATH — must stay non-fatal (adapters may be installed later / on other machines; `ErrAdapterNotFound` at sync remains the enforcement).
13. When explicit `--target` overrides discovery, note skipped discoveries: "also detected .cursor (not enabled)".
14. Staleness: targets are snapshotted; a later-added `.cursor` never joins. A future sync-side hint ("footprint .cursor present but cursor not in targets") or `agent-sync update` rediscovery is out of scope — log it as such.
15. Move the existing-manifest stat before wizard/discovery.

---

## (d) Sync handoff gaps

- **`.agents` absent** → `ErrSourceMissing` hard failure (Gap 4, must-resolve). Empty-but-present dir → soft zero-emit hint. The delta between those two outcomes is the strongest argument for init creating the dir.
- **Discovered target inert at some scope** → already covered: the capability-lie gate demotes scope-skipped kinds for scope-aware adapters (per the resolved scope-aware work, PR #30 lineage), and #37's zero-emit hint covers empty output. No new init work needed, but a test pinning "discovered antigravity at project scope + `sync --user`" would lock it in.
- **PATH adapter targets** → explicit `--target foo` writes fine (no init validation), sync resolves via PATH discovery. Only gap: the zero-match failure/hint message should mention that PATH adapters exist and are never auto-discovered, so `--target` is the route for them.
- **Empty `targets:` manifests** (if Gap 1 resolves toward writing them) → sync already treats this as a valid not-yet-configured state; no engine change needed.

**Suggested next steps:** resolve Gaps 1–5 in the plan doc before implementation (1 and 4 change user-visible contracts; 3 is the largest code delta — the wizard needs a LocalDir concept, a conditional ref phase, and a changed `wizard.Run` signature). Gaps 6–11 are decisions that fit in the same plan revision as one-liners each.</result>

9. Bare `init` → wizard, with discovered targets pre-selected and a `.agents` source default. Requires wizard rework (Gap 3).

11. `init --target claude` on TTY (no source) → **today launches the wizard** (`cmd_init.go:66` gates only on source flags). With a source default, does it now go fully non-interactive with source=`.agents`? Unspecified (Gap 5).

15. `init --dir ~` (home) → probes `~/.claude`, `~/.cursor`, `~/.codex`, `~/.pi`, `~/.agent`. Antigravity's **user-scope** footprint is `.gemini`, not `.agent` (`bundled/antigravity/capabilities.yaml`), so antigravity users are never discovered at home. Conversely `~/.cursor`/`~/.codex` exist for anyone who merely installed those tools — at home that's arguably the right signal, but the asymmetry is real (Gap 7).

---

## (b) Edge cases / state transitions

- **`.claude` is a file, not a dir** → plan says "os.Stat on dir names"; must add an `IsDir()` check or a file named `.pi` fires discovery.
- **Symlinked footprint dir** → `os.Stat` follows symlinks; a dangling symlink is not discovered. Fine, but state it.
- **Stat error other than NotExist** (permission-denied dir) → skip-silently vs fatal? Existing init precedent treats non-NotExist stat as fatal (`cmd_init.go:104-107`); for a 5-dir probe, fatal on one unreadable dir is harsh. Decide and document.
- **Case-insensitive APFS/NTFS**: `.CLAUDE` will satisfy `os.Stat(".claude")` on the default macOS filesystem. Accept as minor; don't try to fight it.
- **`.agents` vs `.agent`**: the source default and antigravity's footprint differ by one character. Users will typo `--local-dir .agent`; error copy and docs should keep these visually distinct.
- **Ordering**: discovered targets must be written sorted (wizard already sorts, `wizard/init.go:79`); a map-driven probe would otherwise produce nondeterministic `targets:` order — noisy diffs, flaky tests.
- **Duplicate `--target claude --target claude`** → duplicate manifest entries. Pre-existing; spec says explicit overrides discovery with no merge, so no new dedup path needed, but worth a normalizing pass while touching this.
- **Wizard with zero targets selected** → today Enter proceeds to confirm with empty `Targets` → valid empty-targets manifest. Fine; keep, but a confirm-screen "targets: (none)" render is needed (currently renders an empty join).
- **Existing-manifest check ordering**: today the user completes the whole wizard before hitting "already exists" (`cmd_init.go:101`). Adding discovery makes the pre-write work heavier; move the stat before wizard/discovery while in here.

---

## (c) Ambiguities the plan must resolve (prioritized)

**Must-resolve**

1. **Item 1 vs item 4 contradiction + breaking change.** In a fresh empty repo, bare `agent-sync init --non-interactive` has no source flags *and* zero footprints — item 1 says it succeeds, item 4 says it fails. Also, `init --non-interactive --source URL` with no `--target` succeeds *today* (empty `targets:` is explicitly spec-valid — `manifest/schema.go:13` "a valid state for a workspace that is not yet configured"), and item 4 would make it fail. **Recommended default:** write the empty-targets manifest with a stderr hint ("no targets discovered; add with `--target` or edit targets: in .agent-sync.yaml") rather than `MissingFlagError`. That preserves item 1, avoids the regression, and the existing zero-emit hint (#37) plus `engine.ErrAdapterNotFound` already backstop sync. If fail-fast is truly wanted, scope it to *bare* init only and say so.
2. **Pin flags × defaulted source.** `--ref`/`--commit`/`--floating` with no source flag must produce a purpose-built error ("--ref requires --source or --local-path; the default .agents source is unpinned"), not the generic LocalDir validation error from `initconfig.go:63-67`. Decide: error (recommended) vs suppress-the-default.
3. **Wizard LocalDir mechanics.** The wizard has *no* LocalDir path: `looksLikeLocalPath` would classify a typed `.agents` as **LocalPath**, sending it into `git.ResolveLocalRef` → failure. "Source screen gains a `.agents` default" needs concrete design: (a) empty-Enter accepts LocalDir=`.agents` (today empty Enter is rejected, `wizard/init.go:118-120`); (b) LocalDir selection must **skip phaseRef** (ref+local_dir is invalid); (c) `sourceDisplay()` and confirm screen must render local_dir; (d) how does a user type a *custom* local-dir vs a local git path — probably a source-type choice, not string heuristics. Also: discovery is fs I/O — do it in `cmd_init.go` and pass results into `wizard.Run` (new parameter), keeping the wizard I/O-free per KTD-4.

Bare init succeeding into a guaranteed sync failure is a bad handoff. **Recommend:** create `.agents/` at init when defaulted (via `fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot` — the CLAUDE.md invariant; reads may os.Stat, writes may not) — an *empty* existing dir degrades to the soft zero-emit hint instead of a hard error. Alternative: print "create .agents/ before running sync". Pick one.
5. **Does `--target` alone on a TTY still launch the wizard?** (Flow 11.) Today yes. If the source default applies, the invocation becomes fully specified and should skip the wizard — but that's a silent behavior change for TTY users. Decide and test explicitly; the wizard-gate condition at `cmd_init.go:66` is the exact line in question.

**Important**

6. **Probe base + bad `--dir` error ordering.** Probe `destDir`; stat `destDir` itself first so a nonexistent `--dir` fails with "no such directory", not "pass --target".
7. **Home-dir semantics.** Either (a) declare discovery project-scope-only and document that `init --dir ~` may under-discover (antigravity's user footprint is `.gemini`), or (b) add per-scope footprints. Given init has no `--user` flag, (a) is the lean answer — but write it down.
8. **Zero-match wizard preselection.** Recommend: discovered set pre-selected when non-empty; **all** pre-selected when zero discovered (preserves today's greenfield UX and makes zero-match non-punishing). "None preselected" + Enter yields an empty-targets manifest one keystroke away — hostile default.
9. **Probe list source of truth.** Derive from `bundledAdapters()[i].Manifest.ReservedPrefix` (already validated, present on all five), not a hardcoded map — new bundled adapters then get discovery for free. Decide explicitly that PATH adapters (`internal/adapter/discover.go`, which carry `reserved_prefix` but require subprocess execution to read) do **not** participate in v1, and say why (no subprocess exec at init).
10. **Accessible mode.** Pin flow 13's behavior in tests: default + discovery apply, silent success is acceptable, zero-match follows whatever Gap 1 decides.
11. **Announce the magic.** Success output must name what was defaulted/discovered: `wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude, cursor [discovered])`. A defaulting feature that prints only `wrote .agent-sync.yaml` hides misdiscovery until sync.

**Nice-to-have**

12. Warn (don't fail) at init when an explicit `--target` name is neither bundled nor `agent-sync-adapter-<name>` on PATH — must stay non-fatal (adapters may be installed later / on other machines; `ErrAdapterNotFound` at sync remains the enforcement).
