content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/justinbeaudry/.claude/plugins/cache/superpowers-marketplace/superpowers/5.1.0/skills/test-driven-development

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" ≠ comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after ≠ TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc ≠ systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code → test exists and failed first
Otherwise → not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.

# Changelog

All notable changes to `agent-sync` are documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

While the project is pre-1.0, minor version bumps may include breaking changes;
the adapter wire protocol follows its own "freeze the frame, grow capabilities"
compatibility policy documented in `docs/spec/adapter-protocol-v1.md`.

## [0.7.0] - 2026-07-04

### Added

- **Cursor skills.** The Cursor adapter now supports the `skill` IR kind,
  emitting to the shared `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>/` tree that Cursor reads
  (project and `~/.agents/skills/` at user scope) — the same tree codex, pi, and
  antigravity co-own, so a skill authored once serves every tool with
  byte-identical `SKILL.md`. See [`docs/adapters/cursor.md`](docs/adapters/cursor.md).
- **`file-leaf` OutputMode + Cursor & Pi commands.** A new engine ownership mode
  lets an adapter own individual files inside a flat directory it shares with the
  user (never the directory, never foreign files; per-file atomic write, drift,
  and orphan reclaim). A pre-existing unmanaged file at an exact target path fails
  closed rather than being clobbered (adoptable via `--adopt-prefix`). Built on
  it: **Cursor `command`** → `.cursor/commands/<id>.md` (project) /
  `~/.cursor/commands/<id>.md` (user), and **Pi `command`** → `.pi/prompts/<id>.md`
  — both flip from unsupported to supported. Cursor now has effective full parity
  (agents-md, rule, skill, command, mcp). See
  [`docs/adapters/cursor.md`](docs/adapters/cursor.md),

content.content:
1178:- [ ] **Unit 17: Bubble Tea + huh wizards for `init` and target selection**
1447:**Post-MVP path:** v0.2 adds cursor + codex + pi adapters (Units 10, 11, 11.5) + validate (Unit 18) + full Unit 6 + adopt wizard (Unit 14). v0.3 adds hooks + watch (Unit 19). v0.4 adds SDK + release packaging (Units 20, 21) + Bubble Tea wizards (Unit 17). v1.0.0 cuts when all of the above ship and protocol has seen at least one out-of-tree adapter survive for 30 days.
16:The architectural essence is a four-stage pipeline — **materialize → compile → stage → swap** — with a **two-mode ownership model** (reserved-subdirectory ownership where the target tool supports it + per-entry ledgered merges into tool-owned files where it doesn't), ledger-driven orphan deletion in both modes, a **two-tier trust model** (committed `trusted_sha:` in `.aienv.yaml` for CI plus per-user `trust.jsonl` TOFU history for interactive work), and a v1-stable adapter extension contract — **LSP-framed JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio** with initialize/initialized lifecycle, capabilities, declared outputs, cancel/shutdown, progress tokens, per-op timeouts, structured errors, and a magic-cookie handshake — so the four primary adapters (claude, cursor, codex, pi) and any out-of-tree third-party adapter (Go, Python, or otherwise) speak the same protocol from day one.
26:- R1 → Units 2, 3, 16, 17 (workspace definition, discovery, CLI + wizard surface)
30:- R6 → Unit 5 (pin-at-init, floating opt-in)
99:3. **TUI: Bubble Tea v2 + bubbles v2 + lipgloss v2 + huh v2** for wizards, with a small hand-rolled stack navigator for the multi-screen init flow. Accessibility: `huh.WithAccessible` + honor `NO_COLOR`.
101:5. **YAML: goccy/go-yaml v1.19.x** with `DisallowUnknownField()` + `AllowFieldPrefixes("x-")` for forward-compat extension keys. `CommentMap` used when `init` writes the resolved SHA back into the manifest.
106:    - **`initialize` → `initialized`** lifecycle (LSP/MCP): server must not emit real ops before receiving `initialized` notification.
108:    - **`capabilities` object** in the initialize result so per-concept support flags evolve without bumping `v1`→`v2`.
109:    - **`declared_outputs` list** in the initialize result (Bazel `declare_file` pattern): adapter announces its full planned output set upfront so the CLI can detect undeclared writes mid-stream and refuse.
114:    - **Magic-cookie handshake** (copying `hashicorp/go-plugin`): CLI sets an env var per spawn and the adapter's `Hello`/`initialize` must echo it, so running `./aienvs-adapter-foo` directly at a shell refuses.
133:16. **`init` offline behavior.** `init` requires network to resolve ref → SHA. `--defer-resolve` writes the manifest without `commit:` and makes the first `sync` do the resolve (which is itself TOFU-gated). `--floating` + `--defer-resolve` behave identically for the resolve step.

109:    - **`declared_outputs` list** in the initialize result (Bazel `declare_file` pattern): adapter announces its full planned output set upfront so the CLI can detect undeclared writes mid-stream and refuse.
114:    - **Magic-cookie handshake** (copying `hashicorp/go-plugin`): CLI sets an env var per spawn and the adapter's `Hello`/`initialize` must echo it, so running `./aienvs-adapter-foo` directly at a shell refuses.
133:16. **`init` offline behavior.** `init` requires network to resolve ref → SHA. `--defer-resolve` writes the manifest without `commit:` and makes the first `sync` do the resolve (which is itself TOFU-gated). `--floating` + `--defer-resolve` behave identically for the resolve step.

content.content:
- [ ] **Unit 17: Bubble Tea + huh wizards for `init` and target selection**

**Goal:** Implement the interactive wizards with every branching state fully specified (fresh/existing manifest, ancestor detected, remote unreachable, local-path mode, zero targets, pre-existing target outputs). Flag-parity with unit 16.

**Requirements:** R13

**Dependencies:** Units 2, 3, 5, 6, 8, 16.

**Files:**
- Create: `internal/tui/wizard/init.go` (multi-screen flow, screen per state)
- Create: `internal/tui/wizard/targets.go`
- Create: `internal/tui/nav.go` (small stack navigator)
- Create: `internal/tui/theme.go` (lipgloss styles, `NO_COLOR`-aware)
- Create: `docs/wizard/init-states.md` (the authoritative branching-state spec)
- Test: `internal/tui/wizard/init_test.go`, `internal/tui/wizard/targets_test.go`

**Approach:**
- Entry preconditions: TTY on stdin+stdout AND `--non-interactive` unset AND at least one decision requires user input.
- Init wizard screens (each a separate Bubble Tea model; composed via nav stack):
  1. Confirm workspace directory (exists or about to be created).
  2. Existing-manifest handling (adopt / replace / abort).
  3. Enclosing-Git-worktree detection + offer `.gitignore` additions.
  4. Ancestor-workspace detection + `--allow-nested` confirmation (distinct screen).
  5. Canonical source entry (URL / local path; mutually exclusive).
  6. Ref + pin behavior (`ref`, `--floating`, `--defer-resolve`).
  7. Remote-unreachable fallback (retry / switch to local-path / `--defer-resolve` / abort).
  8. TOFU prompt for the resolved `(URL, SHA)` (first-URL flavor).
  9. Target selection from available adapters (huh multiselect).
  10. Pre-existing target-outputs preview + adopt-on-first-sync acknowledgment (deferred to actual sync; wizard only warns).
  11. Summary + write confirmation.
- Target-selection wizard is a standalone flow (used by `aienvs target add`/`remove` future commands; v1 uses the block inside init).
- Every screen's text, validation, keymap, flag-equivalent, and per-exit-code state is enumerated in `docs/wizard/init-states.md` — the authoritative spec.
- Use `huh.WithAccessible(true)` when `TERM=dumb` or `AIENVS_ACCESSIBLE=1`.

**Patterns to follow:** Charm's own CLIs; `bubblon`/`bubbletea-nav` stack navigator; Cobra+Bubble Tea integration examples.

**Test scenarios:**
- Happy path: fresh directory → full flow → manifest written with pinned SHA.

content.text: Now the quickstart — updating the init and sync sections:

input.old_string:
## 2. Initialize a workspace

From the directory you want managed, point a workspace at your canonical repo.
Pinning to a commit SHA is the default (and required for a local path):

```bash
# Remote canonical repo (resolves the ref to a SHA for you):
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/your-org/agent-config --ref main \
  --target claude --target cursor --target codex

# Or a local canonical repo, pinned to a specific commit:
agent-sync init --local-path ../agent-config --commit <40-char-sha> \
  --target claude --target cursor

# Or per-repo skills authored right in this repo (no git, no pin):
agent-sync init --local-dir .agents --target claude --target codex
```

The `url`/`local_path` forms write a `.agent-sync.yaml` with a pinned `commit`
and `trusted_sha`. The `--local-dir` form points the workspace at an in-repo
directory (here `.agents`) read straight from the working tree: no commit, no
trust prompt, and it works offline — author skills under `.agents/skills/<id>/`,
rules under `.agents/rules/`, and so on, then sync. On a terminal, `agent-sync
init` with no flags runs an interactive wizard.
input.new_string:
## 2. Initialize a workspace

The zero-flag path covers the common case:

```bash
agent-sync init --non-interactive
```

With no source flag, the canonical source defaults to the in-repo `.agents`
directory (created for you if missing). With no `--target`, init **discovers**
your targets by probing the workspace for each tool's footprint directory —
`.claude/` enables `claude`, `.cursor/` enables `cursor`, `.codex/` → `codex`,
`.pi/` → `pi`, `.agent/` → `antigravity` — and snapshots the result into the
manifest's `targets:` list. The success line tells you exactly what was
inferred:

```text
wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude, cursor [discovered])
```

Zero footprints found is not an error — the manifest is written with an empty
`targets:` list and a hint; add targets later with `--target` or by editing
the manifest. PATH adapters (`agent-sync-adapter-<name>`) are never
auto-discovered — name them explicitly with `--target`.

On a terminal, bare `agent-sync init` runs an interactive wizard with the same
defaults: press Enter at the source prompt to accept `.agents`, and discovered
targets come preselected. (Passing `--target` skips the wizard — the
invocation is fully specified with the defaulted source.)

Explicit flags always win:

```bash
# Remote canonical repo (resolves the ref to a SHA for you):
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/your-org/agent-config --ref main \
  --target claude --target cursor --target codex

# Or a local canonical repo, pinned to a specific commit:
agent-sync init --local-path ../agent-config --commit <40-char-sha> \
  --target claude --target cursor

# Or name the in-repo directory and targets yourself:
agent-sync init --local-dir .agents --target claude --target codex
```

The `url`/`local_path` forms write a `.agent-sync.yaml` with a pinned `commit`
and `trusted_sha`; pin flags (`--ref`/`--commit`/`--floating`) therefore
require one of those source flags — the defaulted `.agents` source is
unpinned. The `--local-dir` form points the workspace at an in-repo directory
read straight from the working tree: no commit, no trust prompt, and it works
offline — author skills under `.agents/skills/<id>/`, rules under
`.agents/rules/`, and so on, then sync.

One naming caveat: discovery matches exact directory names, so the `.agents`
sources dir never enables `antigravity` (footprint `.agent`), and at a home
directory Antigravity is not discoverable at all (its user-scope footprint is
`.gemini`, not `.agent`) — pass `--target antigravity` there.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Quickstart

This guide takes you from nothing to a synced workspace in a few minutes. It
covers authoring a **canonical repo**, wiring a workspace to it, and running
`sync` / `validate`.

A copyable example canonical repo lives in
[`examples/canonical/`](../examples/canonical/). The full, frozen format
contract is [`docs/spec/ir-v1.md`](spec/ir-v1.md) — this page stays at the
"what you need to get going" level.

---

## 1. Author a canonical repo

The canonical repo is a normal Git repository holding your shared agent
configuration. agent-sync reads a closed set of **six concept kinds**, each
living at a fixed path:

```
canonical-repo/
├── AGENTS.md                 # project guidance (agents-md)
├── rules/<id>.md             # one rule per file
├── skills/<id>/SKILL.md      # one skill per directory
├── commands/<id>.md          # one command per file
├── mcp/<server>.json         # one MCP server entry per file
└── plugins/<id>.toml         # one plugin reference per file
```

A node's **id comes from its filename or directory name** (`rules/foo.md` →
id `foo`; `skills/code-review/SKILL.md` → id `code-review`). Ids must match
`[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-_]{0,63}`.

### Frontmatter — the one thing that trips people up

Markdown-bearing files (`AGENTS.md`, rules, skills, commands) accept an
**optional** YAML frontmatter block. Four fields are recognized:

```markdown
---
description: Reviews a diff for correctness and style   # skill summary (see below)
required: true          # fail the sync if a target can't support this node
targets: [claude, cursor]  # restrict to these adapters (omit = all targets)
version: 2              # author-controlled counter
---

The body goes here.
```

> **Describe your skills.** Set `description:` in a canonical
> `skills/<id>/SKILL.md` — it becomes the emitted skill's frontmatter
> description, which is what Claude Code and other Agent-Skills consumers show
> in their skill list. A skill with no `description:` still syncs, but it emits
> a placeholder description and a warning. The skill's `name` still comes from
> its directory id; the adapter generates it. Requires agent-sync ≥ 0.5.
>
> **Gotcha:** other native tool frontmatter (`name:`, `allowed-tools:`, …) is
> still rejected with a hard error — only the four fields above are recognized.
> Unknown fields prefixed `x-` are tolerated (and ignored) for forward-compat.

---

## 2. Initialize a workspace

From the directory you want managed, point a workspace at your canonical repo.
Pinning to a commit SHA is the default (and required for a local path):

```bash
# Remote canonical repo (resolves the ref to a SHA for you):
agent-sync init --source https://github.com/your-org/agent-config --ref main \
  --target claude --target cursor --target codex

# Or a local canonical repo, pinned to a specific commit:
agent-sync init --local-path ../agent-config --commit <40-char-sha> \
  --target claude --target cursor

# Or per-repo skills authored right in this repo (no git, no pin):
agent-sync init --local-dir .agents --target claude --target codex
```

The `url`/`local_path` forms write a `.agent-sync.yaml` with a pinned `commit`
and `trusted_sha`. The `--local-dir` form points the workspace at an in-repo
directory (here `.agents`) read straight from the working tree: no commit, no
trust prompt, and it works offline — author skills under `.agents/skills/<id>/`,
rules under `.agents/rules/`, and so on, then sync. On a terminal, `agent-sync
init` with no flags runs an interactive wizard.

---

## 3. Sync

```bash
agent-sync sync
```

This materializes the pinned content, compiles it through each target's
adapter, and writes native files:

- **Reserved subdirectories** (agent-sync owns the whole directory):
  `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/`,
  `.claude/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`, `.codex/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`.
- **Tool-owned files** (agent-sync owns only a marked section/entry, your
  content is preserved): `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.mcp.json`,
  `.cursor/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`.

In tool-owned files, agent-sync content lives between
`<!-- agent-sync:begin id=... -->` / `<!-- agent-sync:end id=... -->` markers
(markdown) or under `agentsync_<id>` keys (JSON/TOML). Edit anything outside
those — the next sync leaves it untouched.

---

## 4. Validate

`validate` is a read-only dry run — it reports drift without writing. It's the
right thing to run in a git hook or CI to answer "is this workspace in sync?":

```bash
agent-sync validate            # exit 0 = no drift, exit 1 = drift detected
agent-sync validate --output=json
```

Immediately after a clean `sync`, `validate` reports **no drift**. If you (or
a teammate) hand-edit inside an agent-sync-managed section, `validate` flags it
so the next `sync` re-rendering it is never a surprise.

---

## 5. Update — pull in upstream changes

When your canonical source has new commits, `update` moves the pin forward for
you instead of hand-editing `commit:` and `trusted_sha:`:

```bash
agent-sync update              # nearest workspace
agent-sync update --user       # the ~ manifest
```

It fetches the source, shows the old→new SHAs and the commits in between, then
asks before re-pinning and re-syncing. Non-interactively, name the SHA you're
accepting:

```bash
agent-sync update --non-interactive --accept-update=<new-sha>
```

`update` is **fast-forward-only**: if upstream history was rewritten (the
current pin is no longer an ancestor of the new commit) it refuses, and moving
the pin anyway takes the deliberate `--accept-rewritten-history=<new-sha>`.
Nothing is written until you accept — every refusal leaves the manifest
byte-identical. A `local_dir` source has nothing to pin, and `--offline`
declines rather than guessing.

---

## Composing user rules into projects (Cursor)

Cursor has **no user-global rules file** — its "User Rules" live in Cursor's
settings/cloud, not on disk. So a rule you author at the user scope
(`~/.agent-sync.yaml`) can't take effect globally the way `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`
does for Claude. Cursor's own recommended pattern is to put the rule in each
project's `.cursor/rules/`. agent-sync automates that with **composition**:
opt a project in and its sync folds your user-scope Cursor rules into the
project's `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/` alongside the project's own rules.

Turn it on in the **project** manifest (`.agent-sync.yaml`):

```yaml
version: 1
canonical:
  local_dir: .agents
targets: [cursor]
compose:
  cursor-rules-from-user: true   # opt-in; default off
```

With this set, `agent-sync sync` in the project also emits your user-scope
Cursor rules into the project. On an id collision, the **project rule wins**
(matching Cursor's Team > Project > User precedence) and the shadowed user rule
is logged so the drop is never silent.

**Three things to know before you commit:**

1. **Composed files are per-developer, per-machine.** Their content is *your*
   `~/.agent-sync` rules. If you commit `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/*.mdc`, you
   push your personal global rules onto teammates and cause churn when another
   machine re-syncs. Treat the composed output as local-only —
   **gitignore it** (e.g. add `/.cursor/rules/agent-sync/` to `.gitignore`)
   unless you deliberately want those rules shared.
2. **Opting out needs one more sync.** Composed rules are reclaimed on the
   *next* sync after you set the flag back to `false` (or drop the user rule) —
   agent-sync removes what its ledger owns. If you opt out and never re-sync,
   the files linger (and committed ones persist regardless).
3. **`--workspace` does not compose.** An explicit
   `agent-sync sync --workspace <dir>` runs a single scope and skips
   composition; composition only fires on the normal hierarchy-discovery path.

Composition is currently **Cursor `rule` only**. Other tools read their
user-global configs directly (`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`, `~/.codex/AGENTS.md`), so
there is no gap to compose around.

---

## Backing out

A first-class `agent-sync unmanage <target>` command is planned but not yet
shipped. To remove agent-sync-managed content by hand in the meantime:

1. Delete the target's reserved subdirectories
   (e.g. `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.claude/skills/agent-sync-*/`).
2. In tool-owned files, delete the section between the
   `<!-- agent-sync:begin id=... -->` and `<!-- agent-sync:end id=... -->`
   markers (or the `agentsync_<id>` keys in JSON/TOML). Leave the rest.
3. Remove the `.agent-sync/` state directory and `.agent-sync.yaml`.

---

## Try the example

`examples/canonical/` ships as plain files inside the agent-sync repo, not as a
standalone repo. A local canonical source is resolved by commit SHA via
`git.Open` (which opens the directory you point at — it does not walk up to a
parent repo), so copy the example into its own git repo first:

```bash
# Seed a standalone canonical repo from the bundled example:
cp -r /path/to/agent-sync/examples/canonical /tmp/agent-config
cd /tmp/agent-config && git init -q && git add -A && git commit -q -m "seed"
SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

# Then, from a scratch workspace directory elsewhere:
agent-sync init --local-path /tmp/agent-config --commit "$SHA" \
  --target claude --target cursor --target codex
agent-sync sync
agent-sync validate    # → no drift
```

## 3. Sync

```bash
agent-sync sync
```

This materializes the pinned content, compiles it through each target's
adapter, and writes native files.

If a user-level manifest exists at `~/.agent-sync.yaml`, an interactive
`sync` asks whether to include it in the same run:

```text
Also sync the user-level manifest at /Users/you/.agent-sync.yaml? [Y/n]
```

Non-interactive runs never prompt — they sync the project scopes and end with
a note that the user scope was skipped (`pass --user to include it`), in text
output and in the JSON `notice` field, so a stale user scope is never silent.
`agent-sync sync --user` remains the scriptable way to include it.

# agent-sync

[![Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync?label=release)](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases/latest)

`agent-sync` is a Go CLI that keeps AI-agent configuration for multiple tools
(Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Pi, Antigravity — with more planned) in sync
from a single Git-backed manifest. Think of it as `dotfiles` for agents: one
canonical source of skills, rules, commands, and MCP servers; native files
rendered into each tool's own conventions.

> **Status:** pre-alpha. v1 is in active development. Architecture and
> rationale live in the [requirements](docs/brainstorms/2026-04-21-aienvs-agent-workspace-requirements.md)
> and [implementation plan](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md).

## Install

Download the prebuilt binary for your platform, verify its checksum, and put it
on your PATH — one command:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh | sh
```

Installs to `/usr/local/bin` (falling back to `~/.local/bin` if that isn't
writable). Pin a version or change the location with env vars:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/main/install.sh \
  | AGENT_SYNC_VERSION=v0.1.0 AGENT_SYNC_INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/bin" sh
```

Prefer not to pipe to a shell? Download the archive for your OS/arch from the
[Releases page](https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/releases), verify it
against `checksums.txt`, extract the `agent-sync` binary, and move it onto your
PATH. (Windows: grab the `.zip`.) Git must be on your PATH at runtime.

## Quickstart

New to agent-sync? [`docs/quickstart.md`](docs/quickstart.md) walks through
authoring a canonical repo and running your first `init → sync → validate`,
with a copyable example under [`examples/canonical/`](examples/canonical/).

## What agent-sync does

1. **Manifests form a hierarchy.** A `.agent-sync.yaml` binds a directory to a
   canonical source and declares which target tools receive configuration. The
   source is one of: a remote Git repo (`url`) or a local clone (`local_path`),
   both pinned by commit SHA; or an in-repo working-tree directory (`local_dir`,
   e.g. `.agents`) for per-repo skills authored right in the repo — unpinned,
   and exempt from trust and offline-strict since there's nothing remote to fetch.
   `sync` walks up from the current directory (project root = nearest `.git`
   ancestor) and emits every manifest it finds, each to its own scope; the
   user-level manifest at `~` is emitted only with `sync --user`. agent-sync
   never merges across levels — each target tool resolves precedence via its own
   native config hierarchy, and `sync` warns when a scope emits a kind a tool
   won't read natively at that level.
2. **Explicit `sync`.** `agent-sync sync` materializes the pinned content,
   compiles it via per-tool adapters, stages the output, and atomically
   swaps it into each tool's reserved subdirectory (e.g.
   `.claude/rules/agent-sync/`, `.cursor/rules/agent-sync/`, `.codex/skills/agent-sync-<id>/`,
   `.agent/rules/agent-sync/` for Antigravity).
   Emitted skills carry a real `name` / `description` frontmatter block so
   they show up correctly in each tool's skill list, and every managed file
   records its source (`<url>@<short-sha>`) in its header.
3. **Moving the pin: `update`.** `agent-sync update` fetches the canonical
   remote, shows what changed since the current pin, and — on confirmation
   (or `--accept-update=<sha>` when non-interactive) — re-pins
   `commit` + `trusted_sha` and re-syncs in one locked step. It is
   fast-forward-only: a rewritten upstream history is refused unless you
   pass `--accept-rewritten-history=<sha>`. Use `--user` to update the
   `~` manifest.
4. **Safe by default.** Pinning, offline-strict, TOFU on `(URL, SHA)`
   pairs, reserved-prefix ownership with a ledger, and atomic swap with
   rollback — designed to fail closed.
5. **Capability-honest translation.** A tool-agnostic IR (`agents-md`,
   `rule`, `skill`, `command`, `plugin-reference`, `mcp-server-entry`) is
   translated by each adapter; per-target capability reports make
   lossy translations visible.

## Supported tools

Adapters translate the tool-agnostic IR into each tool's native files. Five
adapters ship bundled today; the rest are planned, and the `Planned (tier)`
label records the support tier each will land at.

| Tool | Status |
|------|--------|
| [Claude Code](docs/adapters/claude.md) | ✅ Bundled |
| [Cursor](docs/adapters/cursor.md) | ✅ Bundled (agents-md, rule, skill, command, mcp) |
| [Codex CLI](docs/adapters/codex.md) | ✅ Bundled |
| [Pi](docs/adapters/pi.md) (`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`) | ✅ Bundled (agents-md, skill, command) |
| [Antigravity](docs/adapters/antigravity.md) (2.0 IDE + CLI; replaces Gemini CLI, retired 2026-06-18) | ✅ Bundled (full parity) |
| Windsurf | Planned (experimental) |
| LM Studio | Planned (experimental) |

## Requirements

- Go 1.25+
- Git (shelled out for network operations)

## Build

```bash
go build ./cmd/agent-sync
```

## Test

```bash
go test -race ./...
golangci-lint run
```

## Project layout

See [`docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md`](docs/plans/2026-04-21-001-feat-aienvs-workspace-cli-plan.md)
for the complete repo layout. The short version:

```
cmd/agent-sync/    CLI entry point
internal/          All implementation packages; no external API yet
docs/              Requirements, plans, specs
```

## Contributing

See [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) and [`CLAUDE.md`](CLAUDE.md) for guidance when
working on `agent-sync` itself (including from inside a coding agent). These
files are **in-repo agent guidance for contributors**, not output of the
sync pipeline.

## License

Licensed under the [Apache License 2.0](LICENSE). See [`NOTICE`](NOTICE) for
attribution requirements.

## [Unreleased]

### Added

- **Zero-flag `init`.** `agent-sync init` no longer requires a source or
  targets: with no source flag the canonical source defaults to the in-repo
  `.agents` directory (created when missing, so the first sync soft-lands on
  the zero-emit hint instead of a missing-source error), and with no
  `--target` the target list is **discovered** from the workspace's tool
  footprints (`.claude/` → claude, `.cursor/` → cursor, `.codex/` → codex,
  `.pi/` → pi, `.agent/` → antigravity) and snapshotted into `targets:`,
  sorted. Zero footprints still writes the manifest (empty `targets:` is the
  spec-valid "not yet configured" state) with a hint naming `--target`. The
  success line announces every inference —
  `wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude [discovered])` —
  and detected-but-not-enabled footprints when explicit `--target` flags
  override discovery. PATH adapters are never auto-discovered.
- **Wizard defaults.** The init wizard accepts an empty Enter at the source
  prompt as the `.agents` in-repo source (skipping the ref question) and
  preselects discovered targets (all targets when none were discovered).
- **User-scope sync offer + skipped-user notice.** An interactive plain
  `sync` that discovers `~/.agent-sync.yaml` now asks
  `Also sync the user-level manifest at ~/.agent-sync.yaml? [Y/n]` instead of
  silently skipping it. Any run that skips an existing user manifest (e.g.
  non-interactive) ends with
  `note: user-level manifest at ... was not synced; pass --user to include it`
  in text output and in the additive JSON `notice` field — previously the
  hint fired only when there was nothing else to sync. Declining the prompt
  suppresses the note for that run; `--post-merge` (git-hook) syncs never
  prompt.

### Changed

- **`init --target <name>` on a TTY no longer launches the wizard** — with
  the defaulted `.agents` source the invocation is fully specified. Bare
  `agent-sync init` still runs the wizard on a terminal.
- **`agent-sync init --non-interactive` with no flags now succeeds** (it
  previously failed asking for `--source/--local-path/--local-dir`); scripts
  relying on that failure now get a defaulted manifest instead.
- **Pin flags without a source are rejected explicitly:** `--ref`,
  `--commit`, or `--floating` with no `--source`/`--local-path` now fail with
  an error naming the conflict (the defaulted `.agents` source is unpinned).

## [0.7.0] - 2026-07-04

  `--target` the target list is **discovered** from the workspace's tool
  footprints (`.claude/` → claude, `.cursor/` → cursor, `.codex/` → codex,
  `.pi/` → pi, `.agent/` → antigravity) and snapshotted into `targets:`,
  sorted. Zero footprints still writes the manifest (empty `targets:` is the
  spec-valid "not yet configured" state) with a hint naming `--target`. The
  success line announces every inference —
  `wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude [discovered])` —
  and detected-but-not-enabled footprints when explicit `--target` flags
  override discovery. PATH adapters are never auto-discovered.
- **Wizard defaults.** The init wizard accepts an empty Enter at the source
  prompt as the `.agents` in-repo source (skipping the ref question) and
  preselects discovered targets (all targets when none were discovered).
- **User-scope sync offer + skipped-user notice.** An interactive plain
  `sync` that discovers `~/.agent-sync.yaml` now asks
  `Also sync the user-level manifest at ~/.agent-sync.yaml? [Y/n]` instead of
  silently skipping it. Any run that skips an existing user manifest (e.g.
  non-interactive) ends with
  `note: user-level manifest at ... was not synced; pass --user to include it`
  in text output and in the additive JSON `notice` field — previously the
  hint fired only when there was nothing else to sync. Declining the prompt
  suppresses the note for that run; `--post-merge` (git-hook) syncs never
  prompt.

### Changed

- **`init --target <name>` on a TTY no longer launches the wizard** — with
  the defaulted `.agents` source the invocation is fully specified. Bare
  `agent-sync init` still runs the wizard on a terminal.
- **`agent-sync init --non-interactive` with no flags now succeeds** (it
  previously failed asking for `--source/--local-path/--local-dir`); scripts
  relying on that failure now get a defaulted manifest instead.
- **Pin flags without a source are rejected explicitly:** `--ref`,
  `--commit`, or `--floating` with no `--source`/`--local-path` now fail with
  an error naming the conflict (the defaulted `.agents` source is unpinned).

## [0.7.0] - 2026-07-04

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: zero-flag init (source default + target discovery) and user-scope sync ergonomics" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## What

`agent-sync init` now works with zero flags, and `sync` stops silently skipping the user scope.

**Init (plan U1–U4):**
- No source flag → canonical source defaults to the in-repo `.agents` dir (created when missing via fsroot, so first sync soft-lands on the zero-emit hint instead of `ErrSourceMissing`).
- No `--target` → targets discovered from the workspace's tool footprints (each bundled adapter's `ReservedPrefix` dir: `.claude`→claude, `.cursor`→cursor, `.codex`→codex, `.pi`→pi, `.agent`→antigravity), snapshotted sorted into `targets:`. Exact-name, dir-only stats — `.agents` never matches antigravity's `.agent` (the documented substring-prefix trap has a pinning test).
- Zero footprints still succeeds (empty `targets:` is spec-valid) with a stderr hint naming `--target`; PATH adapters are never auto-discovered.
- Success line announces every inference: `wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude, cursor [discovered])`, plus detected-but-not-enabled footprints when explicit `--target` overrides.
- Wizard: empty-Enter accepts the `.agents` source (ref phase skipped), discovered targets preselected (all when none). Discovery stays in the command layer — the wizard remains I/O-free (KTD-4 parity preserved).
- Pin flags without a source get a purpose-built error; bad `--dir` fails as a directory error before any targets messaging; existing-manifest refusal moved ahead of the wizard.

**Sync (plan U6–U7):**
- Interactive plain `sync` that discovers `~/.agent-sync.yaml` asks `Also sync the user-level manifest at ...? [Y/n]` (never on `--post-merge`/non-interactive/accessible paths; unanswerable prompt = decline).
- Any run that skips an existing user manifest ends with `note: user-level manifest at ... was not synced; pass --user to include it` — text and the existing additive JSON `notice` field. Declining the prompt suppresses the note for that run.
- Fixed the duplicated `<name>: started` lines: the adapterkit session banner (subprocess proof-of-life) leaked to the CLI stderr from bundled in-process adapters, twice per target per sync. Bundled adapters now discard it; subprocess SDK unchanged.

## Behavior changes (see CHANGELOG Unreleased)

- `init --target X` on a TTY no longer launches the wizard.
- Bare `init --non-interactive` now succeeds (previously failed asking for a source).
- Pin flags without a source are rejected with a named conflict.

## Plan

`docs/plans/2026-07-06-001-feat-init-defaults-target-discovery-plan.md` (R1–R18); master plan Unit 17 annotated with the shipped subset.

## Verification

- `go test -race ./...` — all 36 packages ok
- `golangci-lint run` — 0 issues
- `GOOS=windows go vet ./...` — clean
- End-to-end smoke: bare init in a workspace with `.claude`/`.cursor` footprints → announced defaults, discovered targets, `sync` emits skills, no banner noise; plain sync under a home with a user manifest → JSON `notice` present.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/REDACTED
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -2

3	description: Where agent-sync resumes — v0.6.0 released 2026-07-03 (Antigravity full-parity adapter, PR #39 squash-merged e05def1, stamp 8089b46, tag v0.6.0); next candidates are pi command, --break-lock, windsurf/lmstudio/roo adapters; resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/

10	The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
11	orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
12	product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
13	hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
14	enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
15	validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.
16	
17	**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
18	below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
19	`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
20	user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
21	verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
22	(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Antigravity CLI adapter).
23	
24	**MERGED (2026-07-02): PR #38** (squash `b91321a` on main) — branch `feat/skill-descriptions-upstream-update`,
25	plan `docs/plans/2026-07-02-001-feat-skill-descriptions-upstream-update-plan.md`.
26	Two workstreams, 7 units (U1–U7), all committed, full `-race` gate + lint green.
27	**W1 skill descriptions:** `description` is now an authorable IR frontmatter field
28	(`internal/ir/kinds.go`,`types.go`); emitted `SKILL.md` is frontmatter-first
29	(`name`+`description` at byte 0) via a shared `internal/adapter/bundled/skillmeta`
30	helper (codex/pi byte-identical); managed headers interpolate real provenance
31	(`Source: <url>@<sha12>`, replacing the never-plumbed `{source-url}@{short-sha}`);
32	description-less skills emit-with-a-degraded-warning (capability-lie-gate safe).
33	**W2 update:** new `agent-sync update` (`internal/cli/cmd_update.go`) —
34	fast-forward-only re-pin (`IsAncestor(oldPin,newSHA)`) + gate (SHAs+ref+change
35	summary) + atomic `WriteResolvedSHA` + sync, all under one run lock via new
36	additive `engine.Options.RunLockHeld` (avoids the double-acquire deadlock).
37	Exit codes: 4 = needs `--accept-update`/`--accept-rewritten-history`, 6 = pin
38	moved but sync failed. Wire additions (`description`,`source_url`,`source_commit`
39	on node + InitializeParams) are additive/omitempty; SourceURL is always the
40	`cache.Canonicalize` form (no credential leak). Composed Cursor rules carry
41	per-node provenance (resolved open question). **Caveat:** ce-code-review was
42	spend-limit-degraded to an inline audit (subagents kept hitting the monthly
43	limit) — a full multi-persona pass before merge is prudent on this data-loss-
44	critical surface (engine lock seam + manifest mutation). See
45	[[spend-limit-degrades-review]]. Merged via --squash --admin (branch-protection
46	BLOCKED needs admin bypass, same as #36/#37; darwin/amd64 stall + CodeRabbit
47	queue were the only non-green checks — both non-blocking). Resolved 2 gemini
48	`t.Cleanup`-over-`defer` threads pre-merge (94a0481). **The Unreleased CHANGELOG
49	now holds three shipped-but-unreleased changes: #36 Antigravity retarget, #37
50	zero-emit hint, #38 skill descriptions + update — next `docs: stamp CHANGELOG
51	0.5.0` + tag `v0.5.0` release cuts them all** (note: canonical repos adopting
52	`description:` require ≥0.5.0). Remaining roadmap: pi `command`, `--break-lock`,
53	Antigravity CLI adapter.
54	
55	**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
56	— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
57	composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

62	`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
63	`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
64	rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for

84	  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +

97	  antigravity), no engine change. Verified w/ cursor+codex co-ownership sync e2e.
98	- **PR #41 (OPEN, branch feat/file-leaf-output-mode):** the engine `file-leaf`
99	  OutputMode + Cursor command + Pi command. file-leaf = own individual FILES in a
100	  flat shared dir (never the dir/foreign files); reuses the single-file StagedWrite
101	  path (net-new = classification via fileLeafUnder → file is its own `effective`
102	  prefix; per-file drift/orphan ride ScanDrift/AdoptEntries which treat a file path
103	  as a 1-file walk → R10 fail-closed-on-collision for free). Cursor→.cursor/commands,
104	  Pi→.pi/prompts. Cursor now effective full parity. ce-code-review done (4 personas):
105	  fixed fileLeafUnder ./.. guard, mkdir-skip, runtime-gate continue-not-shortcircuit,
106	  + gate/multi-file/adopt tests. DEFERRED (documented in plan follow-up, no bundled

108	  orphan model — files carry "do not edit"), cross-adapter file-leaf co-ownership,
109	  declared-output overlap validation. Codex = no code (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills
110	  by design). Full gate green + real cursor+pi e2e (foreign files survive).
111	- **Deep plan for the rest:** `docs/plans/2026-07-04-001-feat-file-leaf-output-mode-plan.md`.
112	  Cursor `command` + Pi `command` are BLOCKED on a new engine **`file-leaf`
113	  OutputMode** (own individual files in a shared FLAT dir — `.cursor/commands/`,
114	  `.pi/prompts/` — co-resident w/ user files; Cursor CLI ignores command
115	  subdirs so flat-per-file is required). Key design (verified vs code): file-leaf
116	  REUSES the existing single-file StagedWrite path (`fileOutputs`/`fileType` in
117	  `internal/engine/target.go`); net-new logic is classification (a file-leaf
118	  child becomes its own `effective` prefix so `o.Path==pre` routes to the
119	  single-file branch) + per-file drift/orphan that NEVER walks the shared parent
120	  (foreign-file survival = the load-bearing safety prop). Data-loss-critical
121	  (AGENTS.md #6/#7) → TDD + mandatory ce-code-review. Plan is PR2 = engine
122	  file-leaf + pi command + cursor command; U-IDs U1-U8.
123	
124	**Next candidates (in order):**
125	1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
126	   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
127	   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
128	   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
129	   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules
130	   are cloud/UI-only per Cursor staff Nov'25+Jan'26; `~/.cursor/rules/` exists on
131	   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is
132	   required — it does NOT collapse to a remap. Precedence Team>Project>User ⇒
133	   **project rules shadow user rules on id collision**. Seam: CLI-layer node
134	   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
135	   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
136	   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
137	   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
138	   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
139	   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
140	   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
141	   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
142	   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
143	   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
144	   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
145	   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
146	   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
147	   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
148	   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
149	   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
150	   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
151	   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
152	   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
153	   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
154	   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
155	   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
156	   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
157	   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
158	   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
159	   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
160	   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
161	   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
162	   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
163	   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
164	   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
165	2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
166	   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
167	3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
168	   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
169	4. ~~Antigravity CLI adapter~~ **LANDED 2026-07-03** — full-parity bundled
170	   adapter on branch `feat/antigravity-adapter` (commit `b06b2b8`, unreleased in
171	   CHANGELOG). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`,
172	   doc `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`. **Correction to the earlier prediction:**
173	   fresh 2026-07 research + the already-merged PR #36 IR decision both settle the
174	   overlay target on **GEMINI.md** (highest-precedence, tool-specific) — NO
175	   `.antigravity.md` file surfaced in current docs; that earlier note was stale.
176	   Real on-disk split (faithfully reproduced): rules/workflows under `.agent/`
177	   (SINGULAR, Windsurf lineage) → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`,
178	   `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`; skills/mcp under `.agents/` (PLURAL) →
179	   `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi), `.agents/mcp_config.json`. agents-md →
180	   GEMINI.md managed section (NOT AGENTS.md — codex/pi own that). Scope-aware:
181	   agents-md/skill/mcp remap under `~/.gemini/` at user scope; rule+command have
182	   no Antigravity user-global home (coverage.nonNativeAtUser flags them inert).
183	   Verified via unit+integration+real `sync` e2e; go vet / test -race /
184	   golangci-lint all clean. **SHIPPED: PR #39 squash-merged to main (e05def1)
185	   via --squash --admin** (BLOCKED = review-required branch protection; darwin/amd64
186	   runner stall was the only pending check — infra, non-blocking; all other CI +
187	   CodeRabbit green). Resolved 1 CodeRabbit doc nit pre-merge (fcefac9: GEMINI.md
188	   managed-section id precision). **Released v0.6.0 2026-07-03** — CHANGELOG stamp
189	   8089b46, lightweight tag v0.6.0, goreleaser release.yml workflow triggered on
190	   tag. Remaining deferred adapters: windsurf, lmstudio, roo.
191	
192	See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].
193	
194	**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
195	dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
196	flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
197	(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so
198	`sync --target claude` hard-failed (codex/cursor were fixed earlier; claude was
199	not) — now emits inner body like the others; (2) **`validate` falsely reported
200	drift after a clean sync** for tool-owned files — `plan.go` marked every
201	ledgered path WouldUpdate unconditionally. Fix added `merge.DryMerge` (a
202	non-mutating dry-run of the shared merge dispatch that byte-compares merged-vs-
203	on-disk; idempotent merge → no false drift, uniform across markdown/JSON/TOML),
204	plus a CRLF idempotency fix in `renderManagedBlock` (normalize managed-body
205	newlines to the file's style). Added a quickstart + `examples/canonical/`. Plan:
206	`docs/plans/2026-06-15-001-fix-dogfooding-readiness-plan.md`. CI green except the
207	chronic `test (darwin/amd64)` macos-13 hang (its arm64 twin + windows/amd64
208	passed). Deferred follow-ups in PR #26 body: shared-helper dedup
209	(isJSONObject/markerOpenBytes across adapters), readExisting→readFileWithExists
210	rename, stale-slice cleanup for removed nodes in shared tool-owned files.
211	Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.
212	
213	**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
214	shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
215	rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
216	`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
217	`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
218	incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
219	break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
220	`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
221	Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
222	the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
223	newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).
224	
225	**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
226	(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
227	lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.
228	
229	**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
230	adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.
231	
232	The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
233	+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
234	(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
235	change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

---
name: unit-16-restart-path
description: Where agent-sync resumes — v0.6.0 released 2026-07-03 (Antigravity full-parity adapter, PR #39 squash-merged e05def1, stamp 8089b46, tag v0.6.0); next candidates are pi command, --break-lock, windsurf/lmstudio/roo adapters; resume from latest docs/plans|handoffs/
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 9d510688-0061-4983-885f-983207f8530f
---

The agent-sync MVP is **done and shipped** — Unit 16 (CLI command tree + sync
orchestration engine) and the full Phase E landed long ago. As of 2026-06-10 the
product has three working bundled adapters (Claude, Cursor, Codex), production
hardening (Apache-2.0 license, threat model, goreleaser release packaging, an
enforced 80% CI coverage floor), and an end-to-end `init → sync → status →
validate` loop verified against real git + real filesystem.

**v0.4.0 released 2026-07-02** (tag `v0.4.0`, stamp commit `7224ee9`): everything
below through PR #35 is now in a published release — pi adapter, scope-aware
`sync --user` (all 4 adapters), ADV-1 co-ownership + run-lock hardening, and
user-scope Cursor rule composition. Release workflow green, binary `--version`
verified. Next open items are the "Next candidates" list below, items 2–4
(pi `command`, `--break-lock`, Antigravity CLI adapter).

**MERGED (2026-07-02): PR #38** (squash `b91321a` on main) — branch `feat/skill-descriptions-upstream-update`,
plan `docs/plans/2026-07-02-001-feat-skill-descriptions-upstream-update-plan.md`.
Two workstreams, 7 units (U1–U7), all committed, full `-race` gate + lint green.
**W1 skill descriptions:** `description` is now an authorable IR frontmatter field
(`internal/ir/kinds.go`,`types.go`); emitted `SKILL.md` is frontmatter-first
(`name`+`description` at byte 0) via a shared `internal/adapter/bundled/skillmeta`
helper (codex/pi byte-identical); managed headers interpolate real provenance
(`Source: <url>@<sha12>`, replacing the never-plumbed `{source-url}@{short-sha}`);
description-less skills emit-with-a-degraded-warning (capability-lie-gate safe).
**W2 update:** new `agent-sync update` (`internal/cli/cmd_update.go`) —
fast-forward-only re-pin (`IsAncestor(oldPin,newSHA)`) + gate (SHAs+ref+change
summary) + atomic `WriteResolvedSHA` + sync, all under one run lock via new
additive `engine.Options.RunLockHeld` (avoids the double-acquire deadlock).
Exit codes: 4 = needs `--accept-update`/`--accept-rewritten-history`, 6 = pin
moved but sync failed. Wire additions (`description`,`source_url`,`source_commit`
on node + InitializeParams) are additive/omitempty; SourceURL is always the
`cache.Canonicalize` form (no credential leak). Composed Cursor rules carry
per-node provenance (resolved open question). **Caveat:** ce-code-review was
spend-limit-degraded to an inline audit (subagents kept hitting the monthly
limit) — a full multi-persona pass before merge is prudent on this data-loss-
critical surface (engine lock seam + manifest mutation). See
[[spend-limit-degrades-review]]. Merged via --squash --admin (branch-protection
BLOCKED needs admin bypass, same as #36/#37; darwin/amd64 stall + CodeRabbit
queue were the only non-green checks — both non-blocking). Resolved 2 gemini
`t.Cleanup`-over-`defer` threads pre-merge (94a0481). **The Unreleased CHANGELOG
now holds three shipped-but-unreleased changes: #36 Antigravity retarget, #37
zero-emit hint, #38 skill descriptions + update — next `docs: stamp CHANGELOG
0.5.0` + tag `v0.5.0` release cuts them all** (note: canonical repos adopting
`description:` require ≥0.5.0). Remaining roadmap: pi `command`, `--break-lock`,
Antigravity CLI adapter.

**To resume: read `docs/handoffs/2026-07-01-001-hierarchy-composition-handoff.md`**
— the current authoritative restart doc (state + the next task, hierarchy
composition, fully scoped). Plans run through 2026-07-01.

**Latest state (2026-06-30):** All three bundled adapters are now scope-aware for
`sync --user`. PR #30 (merged, `378792f`) made the Claude adapter target
`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` + `~/.claude.json`. PR #31 (**open**, branch
`fix/cursor-codex-scope-paths`) extends it to Cursor + Codex: Codex agents-md →
`~/.codex/AGENTS.md`; Cursor MCP → `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (no sidecar) with
rule/agents-md skipped at user scope (Cursor has no user-global file home for
them — confirmed against official docs 2026-06-30) and surfaced via
`internal/coverage` user-scope gap warnings. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-30-001-fix-cursor-codex-scope-paths-plan.md`. See
[[claude-user-scope-gap]] (now resolved).

**Pi adapter (Unit 11.5) — shipped across PRs (2026-06-30/07-01):**
- **PR #32 (MERGED, 1c62e5b):** pi adapter, agents-md only, scope-aware
  (`~/.pi/agent/AGENTS.md` remap). mcp unsupported by design (no-MCP),
  rule/plugin unsupported. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-002-...`.
- **PR #33 (MERGED, 0cddd9e):** the **ADV-1 fix** + pi **skill**. Shared-subdir
  drift (`ScanDriftUnion`) + orphan + validate/`planTarget` reasoning now union
  all `req.Targets` ledgers so codex+pi co-own `.agents/skills/agent-sync-<id>`.
  4-persona ce-code-review (NO data loss) + resolved 10 PR threads (validate
  mirror, asset prefix-collision guard). Plan: `docs/plans/2026-06-30-003-...`.
  All 4 bundled adapters (claude/cursor/codex/pi) now coexist; pi supports
  agents-md + skill.

- **PR #34 (MERGED, 8327474):** PR3, ADV-1 hardening.
  Per-workspace run lock (`.agent-sync/state/.sync.lock`, `internal/locks/runlock.go`)
  held across `engine.Sync`, closing the cross-process cross-delete race; +
  `warnOrphanLedgers` (non-destructive dropped-target warning; reclaim = future
  `unmanage`). 2-persona review found + fixed 2 P1s: run-lock contention now
  yields a clean StatusBlocked summary (not a hard error) so `--post-merge` still
  exits 0 (Options.RunLockTimeout, hook sets 3s); `warnOrphanLedgers` no longer
  mis-fires on capability-report.json. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-07-01-001-...`.

**Cursor/Pi/Codex parity effort (started 2026-07-04):** Audit found the
"unsupported" notes were STALE for Cursor (it shipped Agent Skills + flat
`.cursor/commands/`), accurate for Codex (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills are
by-design; no code — capability-honesty gate forbids forcing them).
- **PR #40 (OPEN, branch feat/cursor-skill):** Cursor `skill` → shared
  `.agents/skills/` tree (byte-identical SKILL.md, ADV-1 co-owned w/ codex/pi/
  antigravity), no engine change. Verified w/ cursor+codex co-ownership sync e2e.
- **PR #41 (OPEN, branch feat/file-leaf-output-mode):** the engine `file-leaf`
  OutputMode + Cursor command + Pi command. file-leaf = own individual FILES in a
  flat shared dir (never the dir/foreign files); reuses the single-file StagedWrite
  path (net-new = classification via fileLeafUnder → file is its own `effective`
  prefix; per-file drift/orphan ride ScanDrift/AdoptEntries which treat a file path
  as a 1-file walk → R10 fail-closed-on-collision for free). Cursor→.cursor/commands,
  Pi→.pi/prompts. Cursor now effective full parity. ce-code-review done (4 personas):
  fixed fileLeafUnder ./.. guard, mkdir-skip, runtime-gate continue-not-shortcircuit,
  + gate/multi-file/adopt tests. DEFERRED (documented in plan follow-up, no bundled
  trigger): content-aware orphan delete (cross-mode P0 of the existing content-blind
  orphan model — files carry "do not edit"), cross-adapter file-leaf co-ownership,
  declared-output overlap validation. Codex = no code (rule→AGENTS.md, command→skills
  by design). Full gate green + real cursor+pi e2e (foreign files survive).
- **Deep plan for the rest:** `docs/plans/2026-07-04-001-feat-file-leaf-output-mode-plan.md`.
  Cursor `command` + Pi `command` are BLOCKED on a new engine **`file-leaf`
  OutputMode** (own individual files in a shared FLAT dir — `.cursor/commands/`,
  `.pi/prompts/` — co-resident w/ user files; Cursor CLI ignores command
  subdirs so flat-per-file is required). Key design (verified vs code): file-leaf
  REUSES the existing single-file StagedWrite path (`fileOutputs`/`fileType` in
  `internal/engine/target.go`); net-new logic is classification (a file-leaf
  child becomes its own `effective` prefix so `o.Path==pre` routes to the
  single-file branch) + per-file drift/orphan that NEVER walks the shared parent
  (foreign-file survival = the load-bearing safety prop). Data-loss-critical
  (AGENTS.md #6/#7) → TDD + mandatory ce-code-review. Plan is PR2 = engine
  file-leaf + pi command + cursor command; U-IDs U1-U8.

**Next candidates (in order):**
1. **hierarchy composition** (MERGED 2026-07-01, PR #35 = `fd32998` on main;
   squash+--admin, branch deleted) — merge user-scope rule
   layer into project `.cursor/rules/` so global Cursor rules take effect
   (deferred from PR #31). **Go/no-go gate RESOLVED 2026-07-01:** recency-weighted
   research confirms Cursor still has NO native user-global rules file (User Rules

   disk but is NOT reliably read; v3.9 "Customize" is UI-only). So composition is

   injection in `runHierarchySync` (fold user `rule` nodes into project
   engine.Request.Nodes before engine.Sync); ownership falls out via project
   ledger orphan-reclaim. Engine untouched. **Plan written + deepened
   (5-persona ce-doc-review):**
   `docs/plans/2026-07-01-002-feat-hierarchy-composition-plan.md`. Decision:
   opt-in via manifest `compose.cursor-rules-from-user` (not automatic). Review
   verified the provenance-opaque bet is CLEAN (no .Provenance/.BlobSHA in
   engine/sync; ledger Entry = {Path, SHA256-of-bytes}) → U1 spike low-risk.
   Review-added guards folded in: D8 project sync stays hermetic (best-effort;
   never fetch a URL user source, never fail the run on user-materialize
   failure); D3 warns per shadowed id (flat id namespace → coincidental collision
   silently drops user rule); opt-out needs a re-sync to reclaim files;
   --workspace path doesn't compose (known limitation); Rule-of-Three (inline,
   no compose.go/userScopeOf helper). **SHIPPED to PR #35** (11 commits, U1–U6).
   8-persona ce-code-review fixed 2 P1s (empty-Targets leak → pin composed nodes
   to [cursor]; composeActive suppressing warning on soft-no-op → gate on
   len>0) + P3 (cursorRuleIDsOf). Residual P1 (transient user-source failure
   orphan-reclaims composed rules — because .cursor/rules/agent-sync is an
   OWNED SUBDIR swapped wholesale, NOT orphan-deleted per-file) resolved by
   **deferring the whole cursor sync when composeUserRules fails** (drop cursor
   from req.Targets that run; subdir left intact; project cursor edits wait for
   next good sync — accepted tradeoff). Key gotcha for future work: owned-subdir
   swap means "retain from orphan-delete" hooks DON'T protect files; only the
   staged desired-set survives. **Post-PR review round (Codex P1/P2, merged in
   #35):** composition now runs via a shared `applyCursorComposition` called from
   BOTH `runHierarchySync` AND `prepareEngine` — so `validate`, `watch`, and
   `sync --workspace` all compose too (else validate false-flags composed rules as
   WouldDelete drift, and watch/--workspace delete them). `hierarchy.UserScope(home)`
   added. Composition is now consistent across every command that builds a project
   request. Follow-ups still open: wire composition awareness elsewhere if a new
   command builds project requests; the [[capability-lie-gate-scope]] invariants.
2. **pi `command`** — needs file-leaf stage/swap for owned files in the shared
   flat `.pi/prompts/` dir (`sync.Stage` currently `MkdirAll`s a dir leaf only).
3. **`--break-lock` CLI flag** — wire AcquireOpts.BreakLock for both locks (not
   yet surfaced; recovery today = end the holder pid). ledger GC = `unmanage` (Unit 24).
4. ~~Antigravity CLI adapter~~ **LANDED 2026-07-03** — full-parity bundled
   adapter on branch `feat/antigravity-adapter` (commit `b06b2b8`, unreleased in
   CHANGELOG). Plan `docs/plans/2026-07-03-001-feat-antigravity-adapter-plan.md`,
   doc `docs/adapters/antigravity.md`. **Correction to the earlier prediction:**
   fresh 2026-07 research + the already-merged PR #36 IR decision both settle the
   overlay target on **GEMINI.md** (highest-precedence, tool-specific) — NO
   `.antigravity.md` file surfaced in current docs; that earlier note was stale.
   Real on-disk split (faithfully reproduced): rules/workflows under `.agent/`
   (SINGULAR, Windsurf lineage) → `.agent/rules/agent-sync/`,
   `.agent/workflows/agent-sync/`; skills/mcp under `.agents/` (PLURAL) →
   `.agents/skills/` (shared w/ codex/pi), `.agents/mcp_config.json`. agents-md →
   GEMINI.md managed section (NOT AGENTS.md — codex/pi own that). Scope-aware:
   agents-md/skill/mcp remap under `~/.gemini/` at user scope; rule+command have
   no Antigravity user-global home (coverage.nonNativeAtUser flags them inert).
   Verified via unit+integration+real `sync` e2e; go vet / test -race /

   via --squash --admin** (BLOCKED = review-required branch protection; darwin/amd64
   runner stall was the only pending check — infra, non-blocking; all other CI +
   CodeRabbit green). Resolved 1 CodeRabbit doc nit pre-merge (fcefac9: GEMINI.md
   managed-section id precision). **Released v0.6.0 2026-07-03** — CHANGELOG stamp
   8089b46, lightweight tag v0.6.0, goreleaser release.yml workflow triggered on
   tag. Remaining deferred adapters: windsurf, lmstudio, roo.

See [[capability-lie-gate-scope]].

**Immediate state (updated 2026-06-15):** PR #26 **open** —
dogfooding-readiness fix pass on branch `fix/dogfooding-readiness`. Fixed two
flagship-path bugs found by building the binary and running init→sync→validate:
(1) the **claude adapter double-wrapped agent-sync markers** in CLAUDE.md so

Next: merge #26, then resume v1.0.0-gate work.

**Prior state (2026-06-11):** PR #24 (codex +
shared-subdir fix) merged, and PR #25 — the full `aienvs`/`aienv` → `agent-sync`
rename (breaking on-disk + wire format: manifest `.agent-sync.yaml`, state dir
`.agent-sync/`, markers `agent-sync:`, MCP prefix `agentsync_`, frontmatter
`__agentsync_*`, env vars `AGENT_SYNC_*`, adapter protocol `agent-sync/v1`
incl. pkg/adapterkit SDK, cache `agent-sync/repos`) — merged at `810cf3e`. Clean
break, no migration/detection by design. Plan:
`docs/plans/2026-06-10-001-refactor-aienvs-to-agent-sync-rename-plan.md`.
Dated historical docs under `docs/{plans,brainstorms,handoffs,solutions}/` keep
the old `aienvs` name intentionally (history not rewritten). Next action: read the
newest `docs/handoffs/` doc; resume v1.0.0-gate work (pi adapter etc.).

**Known dormant risk:** ADV-1 — two adapters sharing one shared parent
(`.agents/skills`) aren't serialized; activates when the `pi` adapter (Unit 11.5)
lands. Documented in PR #24's body; fix it before/with pi.

**Still out of scope (v1.0.0 gate):** pi adapter (11.5), Gemini + experimental
adapters, the extension-SDK CLI (Unit 20), `rollback`/`unmanage` commands.

The data-loss-critical area is the swap/ownership model: `internal/engine/target.go`
+ `internal/sync/{staging,swap,recover}.go`, governed by AGENTS.md invariants #6
(two-rename atomic swap) and #7 (ledger authority). Run `ce-code-review` on any
change there — see [[spend-limit-degrades-review]].

content.text:
Done — everything is implemented, verified, and shipped as **PR #42**: https://github.com/JustinBeaudry/agent-sync/pull/42

## What landed

**Zero-flag `init`** — `agent-sync init` now needs nothing:
- No source flag → defaults to `local_dir: .agents`, and init **creates the directory** so the first sync soft-lands on the zero-emit hint instead of a hard missing-source error.
- No `--target` → targets are discovered from the workspace's tool footprints (each bundled adapter's reserved-prefix dir: `.claude`→claude, `.cursor`→cursor, `.codex`→codex, `.pi`→pi, `.agent`→antigravity) and snapshotted sorted into the manifest. Exact-name stats only — your `.agents` dir can never false-match antigravity's `.agent` (there's a pinning test for that trap).
- The success line tells you what was inferred: `wrote .agent-sync.yaml (source: .agents [default]; targets: claude, cursor [discovered])`, and notes detected-but-not-enabled footprints when you pass explicit `--target`.
- The wizard got the same defaults: empty-Enter accepts `.agents` (ref screen skipped), discovered targets come preselected.

**User-scope sync ergonomics** (your second ask):
- Interactive plain `sync` now **asks**: `Also sync the user-level manifest at ~/.agent-sync.yaml? [Y/n]` — Enter includes it in the same run. Never prompts on `--post-merge`, piped, or `--non-interactive` runs.
- Every run that skips an existing user manifest now **tells you**: `note: user-level manifest at ... was not synced; pass --user to include it` (text + JSON `notice` field). Previously that hint only fired when there was nothing else to sync at all.

**The doubled `claude: started` lines** — that wasn't your old binary after all; I reproduced it with a fresh build. It's the adapterkit session banner (subprocess proof-of-life) leaking to the CLI's stderr from bundled in-process adapters, twice per target per sync. Fixed: bundled adapters discard it; subprocess adapters unchanged.

## Verification

`go test -race ./...` (36 packages ok), `golangci-lint run` (0 issues), `GOOS=windows go vet` clean, plus real-binary smoke tests: bare init + sync in a footprinted workspace, and the skipped-user notice under a fake `$HOME`. Six TDD commits, plan updated first per repo rules (`docs/plans/2026-07-06-001-...`), master plan Unit 17 annotated.

One heads-up: your `/usr/local/bin/agent-sync` is **v0.4.0** — several releases stale. After #42 merges and cuts a release, re-run the install script; a chunk of the UX pain in your paste was already fixed between 0.4.0 and 0.7.0.
